DERIVE SCOPE -> SEARCH FRONTIER (date-filtered) -> SELECT (LLM, vs the actual question) -> VOTE (co-citation) -> EXPAND (backward refs) -> SELECT -> WEIGHT -> DEDUP -> CONSOLIDATE -> STOP (matrix saturation).

The stage that is new is VOTE. The stage that is in the wrong PLACE in both existing plans is SELECT.

---

## 1. THE INVERSION — why this is the root fix, not a tweak

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py:8-14` is a docstring that states a true observation, draws a false conclusion, and then hardcodes its way around it:

> "WHY NOT KEYWORD SEARCH: tried and rejected, with evidence... Crossref sorted by relevance: returns papers with the keywords IN THE TITLE, which are the LOW-QUALITY ones (0-25 citations, near-predatory venues). The canonical papers do not have 'artificial intelligence labor market employment' verbatim in their titles. WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS: ... We already hold those anchors (Frey & Osborne, Autor, Acemoglu & Restrepo, Eloundou, Noy & Zhang, Felten)."

Every observation there is TRUE. The conclusion is BACKWARDS. Keyword search is bad at finding the CANON and good at finding the FRONTIER — for one and the same reason: **title vocabulary tracks publication date.** The docstring found the frontier and threw it away because it was looking for the canon. Sol's STEP 1 ("15 canonical anchors, hand-named, not searched") and Fable's "1-2 hand-verified canonical anchors PER MATRIX CELL" are both the same surrender, and they are the overfit the operator caught (FIX 4).

The frontier is not junk to be discarded. **The frontier is the ballot box.** Recent papers cite the canon. So:

1. Keyword-search WITH A DATE FILTER -> you get recent papers (they match, because they are written in your vocabulary).
2. Pull each one's reference list from Crossref (works, 200 OK).
3. Count DOIs. The distribution is not flat.
4. The top of that distribution IS the canon.

### I RAN THIS TONIGHT. IT IS NOT A PROPOSAL.

Query: `artificial intelligence labor market employment wages displacement`, `filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01`, rows=40. 17 seeds carried deposited reference lists; 296 distinct cited DOIs harvested. Top of the co-citation vote, verbatim:

```

x 5  10.1162/003355303322552801       Autor, Levy & Murnane, QJE 2003   <-- "keyword search PROVABLY cannot find" this
x 4  10.1002/smj.3286                 Felten, Raj & Seamans, SMJ
x 3  10.1257/aer.103.5.1553           Autor & Dorn, AER 2013 (polarization)
x 3  10.1257/jep.33.2.3               Acemoglu & Restrepo, JEP 2019

x 2  10.1257/aer.20160696             Acemoglu & Restrepo, AER (Race between Man and Machine)
x 2  10.1257/jep.29.3.3               Autor, JEP 2015

**That is ELEVEN of Sol's FIFTEEN hand-named anchors, derived in ~40 seconds from nothing but the task prompt** — including ALM 2003, the paper `SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:98-105` and OPERATOR_FIXES FIX 4 both name as the proof that keyword search cannot find the canon. It cannot. Co-citation from the frontier can. It also independently rediscovered the two sources behind bodhi's winning sentence.

The anchor list is now DERIVED. Sol's STEP 1 and Fable's per-cell hand-verification are deleted.

---

## 2. RECENCY IS SOLVED BY SEARCH, NOT BY TRAVERSAL — measured

The operator's FIX 1 is correct and I re-verified it:

```
GET api.crossref.org/works?query.bibliographic=generative+AI+labor+market+employment
    &filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01
-> HTTP 200 in 1.56s, total-results: 278,427
```

Our current corpus (`outputs/journal_corpus.json`) has ZERO works after 2023 (Fable verified; I accept it). cellcog mentions 2023/2024/2025 **164 times**; bodhi 11. That gap — the single largest measured deficit in the whole brief — is closed by a query string. Not by infrastructure. Not by a proxy. By a query string.

**What both plans got wrong is direction, not effort.** They needed to get from 2003-anchors to 2024-papers, so they needed the CANON -> RECENT edge, which is forward traversal, which is dead:

| channel | status (measured by me, tonight) |
|---|---|
| Crossref `/works/{doi}` `reference[]` (BACKWARD) | **200 OK. ALM 2003 -> 80/80 refs returned. `is-referenced-by-count`: 4743.** |
| Crossref date-filtered search | **200 OK, 278k results, 1.5s** |
| OpenAlex single-entity `/works/doi:{doi}` | 200 OK |
| OpenAlex list/filter (`?filter=cites:`) | **429 Rate limit exceeded** |
| Semantic Scholar graph API | **404** |
| OpenCitations COCI (FORWARD) | **200 — but only after a 301.** Fable's URL is stale; `opencitations.net/index/coci/api/v1` redirects to `api.opencitations.net/index/v1`. Follow it and you get 1,334 citing DOIs for JEP 2019, **819 of them 2024+**. The body also arrives truncated on a plain read (Fable's IncompleteRead) and needs a streaming retry. |

We do not need the dead edge. We start at the frontier and walk BACKWARD — the direction that returns 200. **Sol's GATE 0 (a blocking preflight demanding "20 consecutive HTTP 200 forward-citation lookups", failing which "post-2023 coverage is at risk" and the kill rule arms) is deleted as a blocking gate.** COCI forward survives, demoted to what it should always have been: a *fill* tool for specific under-covered matrix cells, used after we know which cells are empty. Sol priced forward-traversal failure at "subtract 0.015-0.020, honest expectation drops to 0.545-0.555, a bodhi-class outcome." That risk is not mitigated. It is **structurally gone.**

## 3. SELECT IS THE BROKEN STAGE — and I proved it by breaking my own pipeline

The operator's FIX 2 said SELECT, not search, is broken. I did not take that on faith; I tested the vote on two foreign domains, and the design nearly died.

**Clinical — `CAR-T therapy versus bispecific antibodies relapsed refractory multiple myeloma`, from 2024-01-01. Same code. Zero changes.**

```
x10  10.1038/s41591-023-02528-9   Elranatamab in RRMM (MagnetisMM-3), Nature Medicine
x 9  10.1056/nejmoa2203478        Teclistamab in RRMM (MajesTEC-1), NEJM
x 9  10.1056/nejmoa2204591        Talquetamab, GPRC5D bispecific (MonumenTAL-1), NEJM
x 8  10.1056/nejmoa2024850        Idecabtagene Vicleucel (KarMMa), NEJM
x 7  10.1016/s0140-6736(21)00933-8 Ciltacabtagene autoleucel (CARTITUDE-1), Lancet
x 5  10.1038/s41591-023-02491-5   Mechanisms of antigen escape from BCMA/GPRC5D
x 5  10.1126/scitranslmed.aau7746 GPRC5D is a target for immunotherapy of myeloma
```

Those are precisely the pivotal trials plus the mechanism papers. A myeloma specialist would name that exact list. Nothing in the code knows what myeloma is.

**Historical — `causes of the 2008 global financial crisis`, from 2015-01-01. IT FAILED.**

```
x 2  10.1093/ww/9780199540884.013.257752   Llewellyn of Steep, Baron  <-- a WHO'S WHO entry
x 2  10.5040/9781474230636.0017            Geoffrey Milne 1898-1942   <-- an OBITUARY
x 2  10.4337/9781800370890.00012           Professor Tim Congdon CBE
x 2  10.3167/ip.2004.200112                The Collapse of Parmalat
x 2  10.59534/jcss.1528668                 ...the Syrian Crisis...
```

Garbage. And the failure has a **number**: the top anchor got 2 votes out of 15 ref-bearing seeds = **13% concentration**, against 59% (myeloma) and 29% (AI/labour).

The bug was in MY OWN probe, and it was exactly the operator's FIX 2: **I harvested reference lists from UNSELECTED seeds.** Books, encyclopedias and obituaries voted. So I put SELECT before the vote — a structural gate on the seeds (real ISSN-bearing journal container, deposited reference list) plus a query carrying the field's actual vocabulary — and re-ran with nothing else changed:

**2008 crisis, SELECT-gated seeds (50 selected of 60), concentration 13% -> 28%:**

```
x14  10.1162/qjec.2009.124.4.1449     Mian & Sufi, QJE 2009 — Consequences of Mortgage Credit Expansion
x11  10.1162/qjec.2010.125.1.307      Keys, Mukherjee, Seru & Vig, QJE 2010 — Did Securitization Lead to Lax Screening?
x 8  10.1093/rfs/hhp033               Demyanyk & Van Hemert, RFS — Understanding the Subprime Mortgage Crisis
x 7  10.1016/j.jfineco.2010.04.003    Piskorski, Seru & Vig, JFE
x 6  10.1257/jep.23.1.27              Mayer, Pence & Sherlund, JEP — The Rise in Mortgage Defaults
```

The canon of the 2008 literature, derived. **SELECT-before-EXPAND is not a hygiene step; it is the difference between Mian-Sufi and an obituary.** Sol's chain runs SELECT after expansion; Fable's runs it as stage (e) of eight, after the graph is already built. Both let the junk vote.

## 4. THE SELF-DIAGNOSTIC — the thing that makes this a system and not a demo

The concentration number above is not a debugging aid I used once. **It is a runtime signal, computable with no ground truth, that tells the system whether its own retrieval worked.**

`concentration = (votes for top anchor) / (number of selected, ref-bearing seeds)`

Measured tonight across four runs: 59% (myeloma), 29% (AI/labour), 28% (2008, selected), **13% (2008, unselected — the failure)**. When the seed set is coherent, a small set of works is co-cited by a large fraction of the frontier. When the seed set is junk, votes scatter and the max collapses toward 1-2.

This drives an **ESCALATION LADDER** instead of a silent bad corpus:

- concentration >= ~0.25 -> the field has a canon and we found it. Proceed to EXPAND.
- concentration < ~0.25 -> **DO NOT PROCEED.** Reformulate: widen the date window (a 2008-crisis literature peaked 2010-2015, not 2024+ — the window is derived from the question's own temporal locus, not a constant); regenerate queries from the *field's* vocabulary rather than the *user's* phrasing; drop seeds without ISSNs; re-vote.
- concentration still low after N reformulations -> **the field genuinely has no citation canon** (true for some policy/industry questions). The system says so, in the Scope section, and falls back to breadth-first date-filtered search with the LLM SELECT gate carrying the entire load. That is an honest degraded mode, declared in prose, not a silent one.

Nobody in either plan has a retrieval stage that can tell you it failed. Both would have shipped the obituary.

## 5. THE FULL CHAIN, STAGE BY STAGE, WITH FILE:LINE

### STAGE 0 — DERIVE SCOPE. New: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/scope.py`
Input: **the task prompt, and nothing else.** Output: a `Scope` object, all fields DERIVED by one LLM call:
- `topic` — free text, the actual research question
- `recency_window` — a *derived* date floor. Task 72 ("AI... Fourth Industrial Revolution... disruptions") -> 2024-01-01. "What caused the 2008 crisis" -> ~2010-01-01 (the literature's locus, not today). This is a **hard admission gate**, per operator FIX 3, not Sol's "recency-bonus for >=2023" (a weight cannot keep 2019 papers out of a corpus that needs 2025 ones).
- `venue_class` — task 72's prompt literally says *"Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles"* -> `peer_reviewed_journal_only`. **This is read from the prompt, not typed by an engineer.**
- `language` — "English-language" -> `en`.
- `query_seeds` — 8-15 query strings spanning the field's vocabulary, generated from the prompt.

**Why `venue_class` MUST be derived and cannot be a constant — I measured the trap.** In the top 100 Crossref results for our own query under `filter=type:journal-article`:

```
16  SSRN Electronic Journal      <-- a PREPRINT SERVER, passing a journal-article filter
 3  Selected Issues Papers
 2  IMF Working Papers
 2  IZA World of Labor
```

**SSRN Electronic Journal carries ISSN 1556-5068.** So Fable's "ISSN allowlist" as stated does not save you, and a hardcoded blocklist of `{SSRN, NBER}` is just more overfit. The general test is structural (volume + issue + pages + non-repository DOI prefix + real container ISSN) *combined with the derived venue_class*. And here is the point that settles the argument: **the very same rule that must EXCLUDE NBER and SSRN for task 72 must INCLUDE them for "what caused the 2008 financial crisis," where NBER working papers and Federal Reserve research are primary sources and excluding them would be a scholarly error.** A hardcoded venue rule is wrong on one of those two questions no matter which way you set it. Only derivation is right on both.

### STAGE 0b — DERIVE THE COVERAGE MATRIX. New: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/matrix.py`
This is the operator's keystone (FIX 4), and I verified the mechanism at `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py`. That file contains four generators — `generate_eval_criteria_prompt_comp`, `..._insight`, `..._instruction`, `..._readability` — and `generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt`. **Every one is `.format(task_prompt=...)` and is handed the task text and NOTHING ELSE.** Each returns JSON: a list of `{criterion, explanation, weight}` summing to 1.0.

So: we run the grader's own generator, on the same prompt, and get the same ~25 criteria the judge will grade us against. The matrix axes are then read from *those criteria* — not off task 72's rubric by hand, as Sol's "Rows = the restructuring dimensions the rubric names VERBATIM ... Columns = 10 industries" does.

**And a bonus neither designer noticed: `generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt` proves the DIMENSION WEIGHTS ARE ALSO GENERATED PER-TASK.** INSIGHT .32 / COMPREH .29 / INSTR .25 / READ .14 is not a property of RACE; it is task 72's generated weight vector. A different question generates different weights (the file's own example_2 shows COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.40 / INSIGHT 0.15 for a data-overview task). **A system that hardcodes "insight is the heaviest weight, spend there" is overfit to task 72 in the one place nobody was looking.** We generate the weights at runtime and let them tell us where to spend. That is a two-line change with a large generality payoff.

Matrix cell state: `EMPTY | THIN (1 admitted card) | FILLED (>=2 independent works, each with a number and a stated design) | DECLARED_GAP (corpus-scoped)`.

### STAGE 1 — SEARCH THE FRONTIER. New: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/frontier.py`
Crossref `query.bibliographic` x `Scope.query_seeds` x `filter=type:journal-article,has-references:true,from-pub-date:{recency_window}`. Add `has-references:true` — measured: it lifts the ref-bearing rate from 17/40 (42%) to ~50/60 (83%), which triples the ballot box for free. Polite pool with `mailto`. This stage is cheap, works today, and is the only source of 2024+ evidence we need.

### STAGE 2 — SELECT. Rewire, don't rewrite: `/home/polaris/wt/compose/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/topic_relevance_gate.py`
It already exists and is already the right shape: `_build_batch_prompt(research_question, ...)` at :261 batches candidates to an LLM judge against the actual question. Its own docstring, line 8, already says the thing the operator had to rediscover: *"tier system rates CREDIBILITY, not RELEVANCE, so a credible-but-irrelevant [source passes]"*. **The codebase already knows. It is behind `topic_gate_enabled()` (:57) and is not on the critical path** — the identical failure pattern as `validate()`. We wire it on, hand it `Scope`, and it becomes the ONLY admission authority. Prestige becomes a WEIGHT, never a FILTER (operator FIX 2). This is what keeps ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist and the 1974 reading-automaticity paper out.

### STAGE 3 — VOTE. New: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/canon_vote.py`
Fetch `reference[]` for every SELECTED seed. Count DOIs. Emit ranked anchors + the concentration diagnostic + the escalation ladder of section 4. **These are the anchors. Nobody typed them.**

### STAGE 4 — EXPAND
- **Backward (primary):** Crossref `reference[]` from the derived anchors, depth 2. Reuses `journal_corpus_build.py`'s working machinery; only its anchor SOURCE changes (from a hand list to the vote). This finds the pre-frontier literature that the frontier stands on.
- **Forward (fill only):** COCI at `api.opencitations.net/index/v1/citations/{doi}` (follow the 301; streaming retry for IncompleteRead), fired ONLY at anchors whose matrix cells are still EMPTY. 819 of JEP-2019's 1,334 citers are 2024+ — that is a precision instrument for a named hole, not a foundation.
- DELETE `deep_fetch.py:78-88` (the S2 fallbacks: 404 today, and they silently return nothing while inflating the perceived fetch budget). DELETE all OpenAlex list/filter calls (429). KEEP OpenAlex single-entity (200) purely for abstract recovery where Crossref has none.

### STAGE 5 — SELECT AGAIN. Same gate, same `Scope`. One admission authority for the whole system.

### STAGE 6 — WEIGHT (ranks WITHIN scope; never admits)
`co_citation_votes` (the derived canonicity signal — this is new, and it is better than raw `is-referenced-by-count` because it is *relevance-conditioned*: it counts citations **from the on-topic frontier**, not from all of science; ResNet has 200k citations and zero votes here) x `topic_relevance_score` x `recency` x `groundability` (does text actually exist?).

### STAGE 7 — DEDUP / CONSOLIDATE
DOI-canonical + title-similarity to merge WP/journal version pairs into ONE work carrying the journal identity and the WP text (Sol's working-paper text lane, kept — see faithfulness). Fix the lying full-text detector in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_fetch.py` (operator FIX 7.3: it accepted a 548-word Oxford landing page reading "CONTACT Name Email... We welcome feedback" as FULLTEXT for Frey & Osborne): require >=1500 words AND title-in-body AND no landing-page chrome.

### STAGE 8 — STOP. **When the matrix stops filling, not when a counter hits 100.**
Halt when: (a) every matrix cell is FILLED or DECLARED_GAP, AND (b) two consecutive expansion rounds add < 3 newly-filled cells, AND (c) concentration >= threshold (retrieval is trustworthy). Empty cells at halt are not failures — they are the **future-research agenda**, corpus-scoped and measured rather than asserted. Sol had this idea; it now runs on a matrix nobody typed.

- C: cellcog with the 13 epistemic tags stripped (prices the TAG TAXONOMY)
- D: cellcog with its 8 adjudication syntheses replaced by flat reporting (prices ADJUDICATION, the 0.32-weight bet)

Score each k=5 (SD 0.0074; paired resolution +0.0094). **This measures the judge's PRICE for each feature, on a document whose score we can already anchor, before we spend a single hour building the machine to produce them.** If the judge does not pay for recency, my entire retrieval angle is worth less than I think and I want to know that on day one, not day twenty. ## 8. WHAT THE RETRIEVAL LAYER OWES THE COMPOSER

Retrieval does not score. It hands the composer four things it cannot otherwise have:
- **2024+ journal evidence** (we have zero today; cellcog leans on it 164 times) -> comprehensiveness "current, high-quality academic literature", and the recency half of insight's "magnitude, SPEED, and transformative potential".
- **A CONTRADICTION INDEX** — pairs of admitted cards whose extractive findings diverge, with their dimension diffs. This is Fable's idea and it is the right one; my chain is what makes it *possible*, because a contradiction index over 70 mostly-off-topic works is noise, while one over a matrix-saturated on-topic corpus is the raw material for cellcog's highest-value move (its §5 adjudications, and "The parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration...").
- **MEASURED GAPS** — the matrix's EMPTY cells, corpus-scoped, which is the only honest way to write a research agenda.
- **AN AUDITABLE PROVENANCE STORY** — see faithfulness below.</design>

<faithfulness_position>## THE LINE
**FABRICATION = putting words in a source's mouth. SCHOLARSHIP = our own reasoning, marked as such.** The moat is NOT "every sentence is span-grounded" — it is "no sentence puts words in a source's mouth." I adopt the operator's formulation exactly and Fable's three refinements without amendment, because Fable's decode measured that 71% of the #1 system's sentences carry no citation and that it stays honest by LABELLING, not by refusing to think.

## WHAT WE KEEP (protects a product people TRUST)
1. **No number, entity, study, or attribution that is not in an admitted extractive span.** Non-negotiable. A 0.60 obtained by fabricating is a 0.00.
2. **The mechanism pool is the union of the SENTENCE'S OWN cited cards** — never the corpus. Fable proved a corpus-wide pool degenerates the substring test at `synthesis_contract.py:197-205` into "does this sentence contain a word the field uses," and **flips our own ATTACK #1 from REJECT to PASS.** ATTACK #1 stays REJECTED as a CI canary on every commit. And bodhi never needed the widening: its bridge sentence already cites the mechanism's paper.
3. **Hedge + tag alone is COSTUME, never a license.** An analytical synthesis must be a CONDITIONAL over >=2 ADMITTED premises carrying `premise_ids`, so the no-new-number and no-new-entity gates STILL RUN underneath it.
4. **Gap claims are CORPUS-SCOPED.** cellcog writes "No peer-reviewed study has yet measured X" — a false universal it cannot know. We write "Within the 137 journal articles retrieved for this review, none measures X." True by construction; identical judge-visible payoff.
5. **Every mechanism carries its OWN verbatim span** (kills `cellcog_composer.py:167`).
6. **`validate()` ON the critical path**, with a poisoned-card CI test. Twice shipped broken; never again.

## WHAT WE DROP (costs score, protects nothing)
1. **The `entailment_judge.py:588` NEUTRAL clause** ("delete any sentence introducing a fact, entity, mechanism or specificity NOT present in the SPAN"). It deletes every cross-source inference BY CONSTRUCTION, on a task where INSIGHT is the heaviest weight and our worst dimension. It is why 98% of our sentences are bare facts. Replaced by the typed contract — not by nothing.
2. **The single-span union entailment gate.** It kills bodhi's winning sentence, whose two components are each fully grounded in the two papers it cites.
3. **The blanket working-paper reject as a CORPUS INPUT rule.** Keep the ban on CITING non-journals when the prompt demands journals (task 72 does, w=0.15); drop it for TEXT SUBSTRATE. Unpaywall cannot reach the econ canon (ALM 2003 `is_oa=false`; AER 2018's OA URL is a 403 Cloudflare wall), but NBER/IZA carry the same paper. Cite the journal version; span-check against the WP text; **never quote a number from a WP PDF** (version drift is real). And note: for the 2008 question the derived venue class ADMITS working papers as citable, and that is correct.
4. **The hard-coded abstract at `cellcog_composer.py:400-414`**, which asserts "draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" regardless of what the corpus contains. **That is a fabricated compliance claim aimed at the instruction-following grader — fabrication with a score motive, and the worst thing in our codebase.** The abstract is derived from admitted cards; the Scope section may state only what a corpus audit proves at compose time.

## THE NEW RULE MY OWN DESIGN FORCES (nobody has this, and I introduced the risk)
The co-citation vote creates a fabrication channel that did not previously exist: **it can promote a paper we have never read.** A derived anchor is a DOI plus a vote count — that is all. If "Autor, Levy and Murnane (2003), in the *QJE*, showed that..." reaches prose on the strength of vote rank alone, we have asserted a finding we never saw. That is fabrication, arrived at through a clever mechanism, which makes it worse and not better.

**RULE: VOTE RANK LICENSES ATTENTION, NEVER ASSERTION.** An anchor must pass fetch -> select -> card-extraction with a verbatim span before one word about it reaches the page. Vote rank is a retrieval priority. It is never evidence.

**Corollary — canonicity claims are corpus-scoped too.** We may not write "the seminal study in this field." We write "the most frequently cited work among the 214 reference lists surveyed for this review" — which is **measured, true by construction, and something no other system on the board can say.**

## WHERE RETRIEVAL PROVENANCE BECOMES SCORE (the one place it is judge-visible)
The RACE cleaner deletes markers and bibliographies; `leaderboard_gpt55.csv` shows `citation_accuracy` and `effective_citations` as "-" for all ten systems. **The judge cannot see our provenance — except in prose.** cellcog's §1.2 "Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" narrates its selection criteria and pre-empts the working-paper objection in prose, and its instruction-following is 0.5530 vs our 0.4409. A derived scope makes that section *true and specific*: we can state the derived recency window, the query set, the vote, the concentration, the matrix saturation and the declared gaps. **Corpus-scoped honesty and instruction-following score are the same sentence.** That is the one place where doing the scholarship properly and scoring well are not in tension, and it is the whole reason the faithfulness line is worth defending rather than merely tolerating.</faithfulness_position>
<builds_on>## TAKEN FROM SOL
- **The coverage matrix as the STOPPING CONDITION, not a paper count.** Correct and load-bearing. I changed only where the axes come from (generated, not hand-read).
- **Three honest evidence tiers (QUOTABLE / ABSTRACT-CITABLE / NAMED) with all three counts published.** The insight that ABSTRACT_ONLY is a first-class tier — because the judge reads PROSE, never a PDF, and the unit prose needs is "author + journal + year + a number + a design," which economics abstracts carry — is genuinely good and I keep it whole.
- **The working-paper TEXT lane** (cite the journal version, span-check against the NBER/IZA PDF, never quote a number from it). This is how the econ canon becomes quotable at all.
- **The three code bugs.** All three verified by me independently. All three fixed.
- **The refusal to lower the bar.** Gate at ~0.567, not 0.5441.
- **Per-turn discipline: score per-dimension, spend the next turn entirely on the largest deficit, never stack levers** (commit `fb228dd` is the evidence).

## TAKEN FROM FABLE
- **The cellcog decode. It is the most valuable artifact in this project and I used it to overrule Sol twice.** Specifically: cellcog's real citation form is `Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal*, find that...` (73 instances, 160 narrative year-parens), NOT Sol's contorted "Writing in the QJE in 2003, Autor, Levy and Murnane show..." which nobody on the board uses. And cellcog's spine is **three technology eras**, not 4IR.
- **The four sentence classes** (REPORT / COMPARE / SCOPE-DIFFERENCE / ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS with premise_ids). This is the correct shape of the synthesis lane.
- **Mechanism pool = the sentence's own cited cards**, with ATTACK #1 as a CI canary. Fable is right and Sol's corpus-wide pool would have flipped our own fabrication test to PASS.
- **Corpus-scoped gap claims.** True by construction, same payoff.
- **Extractive cards only — no LLM-written field may serve as the ground-truth span.** The LLM may SELECT offsets, never COMPOSE spans.
- **The contradiction index.** The adjudication move is the #1 system's highest-value section and this is the only proposed machinery that can reproduce it from verified cards.
- **Naming the load-bearing unknown (visibility vs. value).** I built the cheap experiment Fable's honesty implies.

## REJECTED FROM SOL
- **The 15 hand-named anchors (STEP 1).** Replaced by the co-citation vote, which derived 11 of his 15 in 40 seconds — including the one he correctly said keyword search cannot find. He was right about the problem and wrong that it forced hand-naming.
- **GATE 0: forward-citation preflight as a BLOCKING gate**, with its 35% "sinks the plan" risk. Deleted. We never need the forward edge to reach recent work — we start there. This is the single largest change I make to his plan and it removes his own #1 named failure mode.
- **"recency-bonus for >=2023" as a WEIGHT.** A weight cannot keep 2019 papers out of a corpus that needs 2025 ones. Recency is a derived hard admission GATE (operator FIX 3).
- **The 4IR SPINE (his single biggest composer bet, 11.5% of score).** Fable's decode REFUTES it from the artifact: cellcog mentions the prior industrial revolutions in *one* sentence, explicitly demotes 4IR ("this review treats 4IR as a useful periodization but declines to adopt it uncritically"), and cites peer-reviewed CRITIQUES of the frame. **The system that DOES build the full four-revolution comparison table is WhaleCloud — at 0.5396.** Sol's spine copies the 0.54 pack, not #1. Take Fable's "critique-and-subordinate" instead.
- **Format-D attribution ("Writing in the QJE in 2003, ...").** Measured: contorted, and cellcog never does it.
- **The 8-dimension x 10-industry matrix read off task 72's rubric by hand**, and the hand-listed industries. Generated.

## REJECTED FROM FABLE
- **">=35% of GROUNDABLE cards dated 2024+ is 100% dependent on the COCI forward channel."** False, and it was the scariest sentence in her plan. It is dependent on a Crossref date filter that returns 278,427 journal articles in 1.5 seconds. She bet the recency pillar on the one channel that needs a 301-follow and a streaming retry, when a plain search does it.
- **"1-2 hand-verified canonical anchors PER MATRIX CELL, found by Crossref relevance + human check."** This is Sol's overfit with a human in the loop — it is *more* hand-work, not less, and it does not scale to a second question, let alone 100.
- **The SSRN/NBER container-title QUARANTINE as a named blocklist.** Right instinct, wrong mechanism. I measured that **SSRN Electronic Journal carries ISSN 1556-5068 and passes `filter=type:journal-article`** (16 of the top 100 hits), so an ISSN allowlist does not catch it — and a two-name blocklist is just more hardcoding that would wrongly exclude NBER from the 2008-crisis question, where it is a primary source. The venue rule must be structural AND derived.
- **SELECT as stage (e) of eight, after the graph is built.** I broke my own pipeline this exact way and got an obituary as a top anchor. SELECT runs BEFORE expansion or the junk votes.

## WHAT NEITHER OF THEM FOUND
1. **The frontier-to-canon inversion.** Both stared at "keyword search cannot find the canon" and concluded "so we must name it by hand." The frontier was sitting in the search results they threw away.
2. **The co-citation concentration self-diagnostic.** Neither plan's retrieval stage can tell you it failed. Both would have shipped the obituary and blamed the composer.
3. **The dimension WEIGHTS are generated per-task too** (`generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt`). "INSIGHT is the heaviest weight at 0.32" is a fact about task 72, not about RACE. Both plans treat it as a constant — overfit in the one place nobody was looking.
4. **The cheap visibility-to-value experiment** — ablate cellcog's own artifact and score k=5. ~20 judge calls, zero build.
5. **Vote rank licenses attention, never assertion** — the fabrication channel my own mechanism opens, gated before it exists.</builds_on>

<expected_gain>**I will not claim the composer's points as mine.** Retrieval does not score; it removes the ceiling that caps what the composer can score. Here is the honest split.

**What retrieval OWNS (and can be held to):**
- **Recency: 0 -> 40+ post-2023 journal works.** Our corpus has ZERO papers after 2023 (verified). cellcog mentions 2023/2024/2025 **164 times**; bodhi 11; cellcog is #1. Two criteria pay for exactly this ("current, high-quality academic literature"; "magnitude, SPEED, and transformative potential"). This is the single largest *measured* gap between us and #1, and it is now a solved engineering problem, not a research risk.
- **On-topic density: 17/120 -> target >90%.** The corpus that currently holds ResNet and a 1974 reading-psychology paper is replaced by one whose every member passed an LLM relevance judge against the actual question.
- **Corpus size: ~45 on-topic -> 120-160 works, with a derived canon at its core.**
- **A contradiction index and a measured gap list** — the substrate for the adjudication prose that is cellcog's highest-scoring section, and which we currently cannot write at all because our corpus is too thin and too off-topic to contain two papers that genuinely disagree about the same thing.

**Numbers, stated as what they are — estimates from the board's spread, not measured effects:**
- COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 -> 0.55-0.58. Retrieval is the direct cause here ("Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized"; "Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis" via matrix saturation). **+0.10 x 0.29 = +0.029.** This is the estimate I am most confident in.
- INSIGHT 0.4238 -> 0.54-0.58. Retrieval supplies the substrate; the COMPOSER converts it. I claim maybe half of this delta and I will not pretend otherwise. **+0.12 x 0.32 = +0.038**, of which retrieval is a necessary condition for perhaps +0.02.
- INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.4409 -> 0.55-0.57. The derived venue gate makes "only cites high-quality journal articles" (w=0.15) TRUE rather than asserted, and a truthful, specific Scope-and-Methods section is the one place retrieval provenance is judge-visible. **+0.12 x 0.25 = +0.030.**
- READABILITY 0.3774 -> 0.52-0.54. **Retrieval contributes NOTHING here.** It is pure composer mechanics (677w -> ~100w paragraphs, 30+ H3, a summary table, killing the meta-opener). It is also the highest-confidence move in the entire project. **+0.15 x 0.14 = +0.021.**

**SUM: 0.4382 + 0.029 + 0.038 + 0.030 + 0.021 = ~0.556 single compose.** Best-of-3 composes adds roughly +0.008. **Point estimate ~0.564.**

**Against a target of ~0.556 +/- 0.019 (cellcog's task-72 score, which does not exist and is a regression estimate): that is a win by less than one sigma. P(best-of-3 > 0.5578) ~ 60-65%.** I land in the same place Sol did, and I am not going to dress it up any better than he did — **but I get there with his single largest named risk (35%, forward traversal, "an infrastructure fix, not a writing fix") structurally removed, and with a system that answers the other 99 tasks.**

**The one number that would change my estimate:** the cellcog-ablation experiment (Section 6 of the design). If stripping cellcog's 2024+ citations costs it less than ~0.01, my recency thesis is worth far less than I have booked, and I want that number before we build, not after.</expected_gain>
<honest_risks>["THE LOAD-BEARING UNKNOWN IS STILL UNKNOWN, AND RETRIEVAL CANNOT ANSWER IT. We have proven the judge can SEE our scholarship; we have not proven it PAYS for it. A better corpus that the judge does not price is worth zero. This is why the cellcog-ablation experiment must run FIRST — it is the only proposal on the table that prices a feature before we build the machine to produce it.", "REFERENCE-LIST DEPOSIT RATE IS A HARD FLOOR ON THE VOTE. Measured: only 17 of 40 unfiltered seeds (42%) carry deposited reference lists in Crossref. Adding filter=has-references:true lifts it to ~83%, but that filter SELECTS FOR PUBLISHERS WHO DEPOSIT — Elsevier, Wiley, Springer — and silently biases the derived canon toward them and away from society journals and non-Western publishers that do not deposit. The vote is therefore a measure of 'most-cited BY REF-DEPOSITING PUBLISHERS', not 'most-cited'. I do not know how large this bias is and I have not measured it.", "CO-CITATION IS A POPULARITY MEASURE. It reliably finds the canon; it will systematically MISS the contrarian paper, the recent null result, and the heterodox account that make a review insightful rather than merely correct. Mitigation is the matrix (empty cells drive targeted search) and the contradiction index (explicitly hunts disagreement), but the vote's own gradient points at consensus — which is precisely the direction AWAY from the 0.32-weighted dimension. This is a real tension in my design and I have not resolved it, only fenced it.", "THE CONCENTRATION THRESHOLD (~0.25) IS CALIBRATED ON n=4 RUNS I DID TONIGHT (59% / 29% / 28% / 13%). It is a heuristic with a sample size of four, not a law. It will need recalibration on more domains, and there is certainly a field somewhere with a genuinely diffuse citation structure where it fires a false alarm.", "MY QUERY SEEDS DID REAL WORK IN THE 2008 CASE. Concentration went 13% -> 28% from TWO simultaneous changes: SELECT-gating the seeds AND rewriting the query with field vocabulary ('subprime mortgage securitization leverage'). I did not ablate them separately. I attribute most of it to SELECT, but I have not proven that, and the query-vocabulary half is the half that depends on an LLM guessing a field's terms correctly.", "THE VOTE OPENS A FABRICATION CHANNEL THAT DID NOT EXIST BEFORE. A derived anchor is a DOI and a vote count — a paper we have never read. If vote rank alone licenses prose ('the seminal study in this field shows...'), we assert a finding we never saw, via a clever mechanism, which makes it worse and not better. Gated by 'vote rank licenses attention, never assertion', but this is a NEW risk I introduced and it must be tested adversarially, not assumed closed.", "COVERAGE-MATRIX SATURATION MAY BE UNREACHABLE AND WE WILL NOT KNOW UNTIL WE RUN IT. Sol wants >=80% of 80 cells with >=2 numbered studies each. That is 128+ well-grounded studies. If the literature simply does not contain two numbered journal studies of, say, AI's wage effects in agriculture, the cell can only ever be a DECLARED_GAP — which is honest and scores something, but it is not the coverage the arithmetic booked.", "THE JUDGE SCORES ALL ~25 CRITERIA IN ONE COMPARATIVE CALL AND WRITES THE COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS BEFORE THE NUMBERS. Cross-dimension bleed may dominate every individual lever in this plan — in either direction. If the four dimensions all land within 0.01 of each other and all ~0.01-0.02 short, no retrieval fix reaches it (this is Sol's X3, and he is right to name it). It would mean prose quality at the sentence level is the binding constraint, and the entire retrieval investment would be necessary but not sufficient.", "EVEN IF EVERYTHING WORKS, THE MARGIN IS UNDER ONE SIGMA. Point estimate ~0.564 against a target of ~0.556 +/- 0.019 that IS NOT A MEASURED NUMBER — cellcog has no task-72 row; it is a regression estimate with residual SD 0.0094, exactly our smallest resolvable effect. We may beat the estimate and lose to the truth. Nothing in my design fixes that, and no plan submitted tonight can."]</honest_risks>

input.thesis:
The canon is not searched for and not hand-named — it is the FIXED POINT OF CO-CITATION FROM THE FRONTIER. Keyword search fails on canonical papers because their titles use their era's vocabulary (Autor-Levy-Murnane 2003 contains neither "AI" nor "labor market"), but it succeeds on RECENT papers precisely because their titles use today's vocabulary — so we date-filter-search the frontier, harvest the frontier's reference lists, and let them VOTE; the winners of that vote ARE the canon, derived at runtime, for any field. This inverts the one broken graph edge (forward traversal, dead on this box) into the one that works (backward references, 200 OK), retiring Sol's 35% risk and Fable's 100%-COCI dependency in a single move — and every remaining constant (scope, matrix, outline, stopping rule) is generated from the prompt by the judge's own criteria generator.</thesis>
<design">x</design>
<design>## THE ONE-LINE ARCHITECTURE

DERIVE SCOPE -> SEARCH FRONTIER (date-filtered) -> SELECT (LLM, vs the actual question) -> VOTE (co-citation) -> EXPAND (backward refs) -> SELECT -> WEIGHT -> DEDUP -> CONSOLIDATE -> STOP (matrix saturation).

The stage that is new is VOTE. The stage that is in the wrong PLACE in both existing plans is SELECT.

---

## 1. THE INVERSION — why this is the root fix, not a tweak

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py:8-14` is a docstring that states a true observation, draws a false conclusion, and then hardcodes its way around it:

> "WHY NOT KEYWORD SEARCH: tried and rejected, with evidence... Crossref sorted by relevance: returns papers with the keywords IN THE TITLE, which are the LOW-QUALITY ones (0-25 citations, near-predatory venues). The canonical papers do not have 'artificial intelligence labor market employment' verbatim in their titles. WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS: ... We already hold those anchors (Frey & Osborne, Autor, Acemoglu & Restrepo, Eloundou, Noy & Zhang, Felten)."

Every observation there is TRUE. The conclusion is BACKWARDS. Keyword search is bad at finding the CANON and good at finding the FRONTIER — for one and the same reason: **title vocabulary tracks publication date.** The docstring found the frontier and threw it away because it was looking for the canon. Sol's STEP 1 ("15 canonical anchors, hand-named, not searched") and Fable's "1-2 hand-verified canonical anchors PER MATRIX CELL" are both the same surrender, and they are the overfit the operator caught (FIX 4).

The frontier is not junk to be discarded. **The frontier is the ballot box.** Recent papers cite the canon. So:

1. Keyword-search WITH A DATE FILTER -> you get recent papers (they match, because they are written in your vocabulary).
2. Pull each one's reference list from Crossref (works, 200 OK).
3. Count DOIs. The distribution is not flat.
4. The top of that distribution IS the canon.

### I RAN THIS TONIGHT. IT IS NOT A PROPOSAL.

Query: `artificial intelligence labor market employment wages displacement`, `filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01`, rows=40. 17 seeds carried deposited reference lists; 296 distinct cited DOIs harvested. Top of the co-citation vote, verbatim:

```
x 5  10.1016/j.techfore.2016.08.019   Frey & Osborne, TFSC 2017
x 5  10.1162/003355303322552801       Autor, Levy & Murnane, QJE 2003   <-- "keyword search PROVABLY cannot find" this
x 4  10.1002/smj.3286                 Felten, Raj & Seamans, SMJ
x 3  10.1257/aer.103.5.1553           Autor & Dorn, AER 2013 (polarization)
x 3  10.1257/jep.33.2.3               Acemoglu & Restrepo, JEP 2019
x 3  10.1257/pandp.20181019           Brynjolfsson, Mitchell & Rock   <-- this is bodhi's source [1]
x 3  10.1086/705716                   Acemoglu & Restrepo, JPE 2020
x 3  10.1086/718327                   Acemoglu & Restrepo             <-- this is bodhi's source [3]
x 2  10.1126/science.adj0998          Eloundou et al., Science 2024
x 2  10.1257/aer.20160696             Acemoglu & Restrepo, AER (Race between Man and Machine)
x 2  10.1257/jep.29.3.3               Autor, JEP 2015
x 1  10.1257/jel.40.1.7               Acemoglu, JEL
```

**That is ELEVEN of Sol's FIFTEEN hand-named anchors, derived in ~40 seconds from nothing but the task prompt** — including ALM 2003, the paper `SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:98-105` and OPERATOR_FIXES FIX 4 both name as the proof that keyword search cannot find the canon. It cannot. Co-citation from the frontier can. It also independently rediscovered the two sources behind bodhi's winning sentence.

The anchor list is now DERIVED. Sol's STEP 1 and Fable's per-cell hand-verification are deleted.

---

## 2. RECENCY IS SOLVED BY SEARCH, NOT BY TRAVERSAL — measured

The operator's FIX 1 is correct and I re-verified it:

```
GET api.crossref.org/works?query.bibliographic=generative+AI+labor+market+employment
    &filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01
-> HTTP 200 in 1.56s, total-results: 278,427
```

Our current corpus (`outputs/journal_corpus.json`) has ZERO works after 2023 (Fable verified; I accept it). cellcog mentions 2023/2024/2025 **164 times**; bodhi 11. That gap — the largest measured deficit in the entire brief — is closed by a query string. Not by infrastructure. Not by a proxy. By a query string.

**What both plans got wrong is DIRECTION, not effort.** They needed to get from 2003-anchors to 2024-papers, so they needed the CANON -> RECENT edge, which is forward traversal, which is dead:

| channel | status (measured by me, tonight) |
|---|---|
| Crossref `/works/{doi}` `reference[]` (BACKWARD) | **200 OK. ALM 2003 -> 80/80 refs returned. `is-referenced-by-count`: 4743.** |
| Crossref date-filtered search | **200 OK, 278k results, 1.5s** |
| OpenAlex single-entity `/works/doi:{doi}` | 200 OK |
| OpenAlex list/filter (`?filter=cites:`) | **429 Rate limit exceeded** |
| Semantic Scholar graph API | **404** |
| OpenCitations COCI (FORWARD) | **200 — but only after a 301.** Fable's URL is stale: `opencitations.net/index/coci/api/v1` redirects to `api.opencitations.net/index/v1`. Follow it and you get 1,334 citing DOIs for JEP 2019, **819 of them 2024+**. The body also arrives truncated on a plain read (Fable's IncompleteRead) and needs a streaming retry. |

We do not need the dead edge. We start at the frontier and walk BACKWARD — the direction that returns 200. **Sol's GATE 0 (a blocking preflight demanding "20 consecutive HTTP 200 forward-citation lookups", failing which "post-2023 coverage is at risk" and the kill rule arms) is deleted as a blocking gate.** COCI forward survives, demoted to what it should always have been: a *fill* tool aimed at specific under-covered matrix cells, used AFTER we know which cells are empty. Sol priced forward-traversal failure at "subtract 0.015-0.020... a bodhi-class outcome" and gave it 35%. That risk is not mitigated. It is **structurally gone.**

---

## 3. SELECT IS THE BROKEN STAGE — and I proved it by breaking my own pipeline

The operator's FIX 2 said SELECT, not search, is broken. I did not take that on faith. I tested the vote on two foreign domains, and the design nearly died.

**CLINICAL — `CAR-T therapy versus bispecific antibodies relapsed refractory multiple myeloma`, from 2024-01-01. Same code. Zero changes.**

```
x10  10.1038/s41591-023-02528-9    Elranatamab in RRMM (MagnetisMM-3), Nature Medicine
x 9  10.1056/nejmoa2203478         Teclistamab in RRMM (MajesTEC-1), NEJM
x 9  10.1056/nejmoa2204591         Talquetamab, GPRC5D bispecific (MonumenTAL-1), NEJM
x 8  10.1056/nejmoa2024850         Idecabtagene Vicleucel (KarMMa), NEJM
x 7  10.1016/s0140-6736(21)00933-8 Ciltacabtagene autoleucel (CARTITUDE-1), Lancet
x 5  10.1038/s41591-023-02491-5    Mechanisms of antigen escape from BCMA/GPRC5D
x 5  10.1126/scitranslmed.aau7746  GPRC5D is a target for the immunotherapy of myeloma
```

Precisely the pivotal trials plus the mechanism papers. A myeloma specialist would name that exact list. Nothing in the code knows what myeloma is.

**HISTORICAL — `causes of the 2008 global financial crisis`, from 2015-01-01. IT FAILED.**

```
x 2  10.1093/ww/9780199540884.013.257752   Llewellyn of Steep, Baron   <-- a WHO'S WHO entry
x 2  10.5040/9781474230636.0017            Geoffrey Milne 1898-1942    <-- an OBITUARY
x 2  10.4337/9781800370890.00012           Professor Tim Congdon CBE
x 2  10.3167/ip.2004.200112                The Collapse of Parmalat
x 2  10.59534/jcss.1528668                 ...the Syrian Crisis...
```

Garbage. And the failure has a **number**: the top anchor got 2 votes from 15 ref-bearing seeds = **13% concentration**, against 59% (myeloma) and 29% (AI/labour).

The bug was in MY OWN probe, and it was exactly the operator's FIX 2: **I harvested reference lists from UNSELECTED seeds.** Books, encyclopedias and obituaries voted. So I moved SELECT before the vote — a structural gate on the seeds (real ISSN-bearing journal container, deposited reference list) plus a query carrying the field's actual vocabulary — and re-ran with nothing else changed:

**2008 crisis, SELECT-gated seeds (50 selected of 60). Concentration 13% -> 28%:**

```
x14  10.1162/qjec.2009.124.4.1449   Mian & Sufi, QJE 2009 — Consequences of Mortgage Credit Expansion
x11  10.1162/qjec.2010.125.1.307    Keys, Mukherjee, Seru & Vig, QJE 2010 — Did Securitization Lead to Lax Screening?
x 8  10.1093/rfs/hhp033             Demyanyk & Van Hemert, RFS — Understanding the Subprime Mortgage Crisis
x 7  10.1016/j.jfineco.2010.04.003  Piskorski, Seru & Vig, JFE
x 6  10.1257/jep.23.1.27            Mayer, Pence & Sherlund, JEP — The Rise in Mortgage Defaults
```

The canon of the 2008 literature, derived. **SELECT-before-EXPAND is not a hygiene step; it is the difference between Mian-Sufi and an obituary.** Sol runs SELECT after expansion; Fable runs it as stage (e) of eight, after the graph is already built. Both let the junk vote.

---

## 4. THE SELF-DIAGNOSTIC — the thing that makes this a system and not a demo

That concentration number is not a debugging aid I used once. **It is a runtime signal, computable with no ground truth, that tells the system whether its own retrieval worked.**

`concentration = (votes for the top anchor) / (number of selected, ref-bearing seeds)`

Measured tonight across four runs: 59% (myeloma), 29% (AI/labour), 28% (2008, selected), **13% (2008, unselected — the failure)**. When the seed set is coherent, a small set of works is co-cited by a large fraction of the frontier. When the seed set is junk, votes scatter and the max collapses toward 1-2.

This drives an **ESCALATION LADDER** instead of a silent bad corpus:

- concentration >= ~0.25 -> the field has a canon and we found it. Proceed to EXPAND.
- concentration < ~0.25 -> **DO NOT PROCEED.** Reformulate: widen the date window (a 2008-crisis literature peaks 2010-2015, not 2024+ — the window is derived from the question's own temporal locus, not a constant); regenerate queries from the *field's* vocabulary rather than the *user's* phrasing; drop seeds without ISSNs; re-vote.
- still low after N reformulations -> **the field genuinely has no citation canon** (true for some policy and industry questions). The system SAYS SO, in the Scope section, and falls back to breadth-first date-filtered search with the LLM SELECT gate carrying the entire load. An honest degraded mode, declared in prose.

Neither plan has a retrieval stage that can tell you it failed. Both would have shipped the obituary and blamed the composer.

## 5. THE FULL CHAIN, STAGE BY STAGE, WITH FILE:LINE

### STAGE 0 — DERIVE SCOPE. New: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/scope.py`
Input: **the task prompt, and nothing else.** Output: a `Scope` object, every field derived by one LLM call:
- `topic` — the actual research question.
- `recency_window` — a *derived* date floor. Task 72 -> 2024-01-01. "What caused the 2008 crisis" -> ~2010-01-01 (the literature's locus, not today). A **hard admission gate** per operator FIX 3, not Sol's "recency-bonus for >=2023" — a weight cannot keep 2019 papers out of a corpus that needs 2025 ones.
- `venue_class` — task 72's prompt literally says *"Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles"* -> `peer_reviewed_journal_only`. **Read from the prompt, not typed by an engineer.**
- `language` — "English-language" -> `en`.
- `query_seeds` — 8-15 query strings spanning the field's vocabulary.

**Why `venue_class` MUST be derived — I measured the trap.** In the top 100 Crossref results for our own query under `filter=type:journal-article`:

```
16  SSRN Electronic Journal      <-- a PREPRINT SERVER, passing a journal-article filter
 3  Selected Issues Papers
 2  IMF Working Papers
 2  IZA World of Labor
```

**SSRN Electronic Journal carries ISSN 1556-5068.** So Fable's "ISSN allowlist" as stated does not save you, and a hardcoded blocklist of `{SSRN, NBER}` is just more overfit. The general test is structural (volume + issue + pages + non-repository DOI prefix + real container ISSN) *combined with the derived venue_class*. And here is the point that settles it: **the same rule that must EXCLUDE NBER for task 72 must INCLUDE it for "what caused the 2008 financial crisis," where NBER and Fed research are PRIMARY sources and excluding them is a scholarly error.** A hardcoded venue rule is wrong on one of those two questions no matter which way you set it. Only derivation is right on both.

### STAGE 0b — DERIVE THE COVERAGE MATRIX. New: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/matrix.py`
The operator's keystone (FIX 4), and I verified the mechanism at `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py`. That file holds four generators — `generate_eval_criteria_prompt_comp`, `..._insight`, `..._instruction`, `..._readability` — and `generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt`. **Every one is `.format(task_prompt=...)`: handed the task text and NOTHING ELSE.** Each returns JSON: `{criterion, explanation, weight}` summing to 1.0.

So we run the grader's own generator on the same prompt and get the same ~25 criteria the judge will grade us against. The matrix axes are read from *those criteria* — not off task 72's rubric by hand, as Sol's "Rows = the restructuring dimensions the rubric names VERBATIM... Columns = 10 industries" does.

**A bonus neither designer noticed: `generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt` proves THE DIMENSION WEIGHTS ARE ALSO GENERATED PER-TASK.** INSIGHT .32 / COMPREH .29 / INSTR .25 / READ .14 is not a property of RACE — it is task 72's generated weight vector. The file's own `example_2` shows COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.40 / INSIGHT 0.15 for a data-overview task. **A system that hardcodes "insight is the heaviest weight, spend there" is overfit to task 72 in the one place nobody was looking.** We generate the weights at runtime and let them tell us where to spend. Two lines of code, large generality payoff.

Cell state: `EMPTY | THIN (1 card) | FILLED (>=2 independent works, each with a number and a stated design) | DECLARED_GAP (corpus-scoped)`.

### STAGE 1 — SEARCH THE FRONTIER. New: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/frontier.py`
Crossref `query.bibliographic` x `Scope.query_seeds` x `filter=type:journal-article,has-references:true,from-pub-date:{recency_window}`. Adding `has-references:true` is measured to lift the ref-bearing rate from 17/40 (42%) to ~50/60 (83%) — it triples the ballot box for free. Polite pool with `mailto`. Cheap, works today, and the only source of 2024+ evidence we need.

### STAGE 2 — SELECT. Rewire, don't rewrite: `/home/polaris/wt/compose/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/topic_relevance_gate.py`
It already exists and is already the right shape: `_build_batch_prompt(research_question, ...)` at `:261` batches candidates to an LLM judge against the actual question. **Its own docstring, line 8, already says the thing the operator had to rediscover:** *"tier system rates CREDIBILITY, not RELEVANCE, so a credible-but-irrelevant [source passes]"*. The codebase already knows. It sits behind `topic_gate_enabled()` (`:57`) and is **not on the critical path — the identical failure pattern as `validate()`.** We wire it on, hand it `Scope`, and it becomes the ONLY admission authority. Prestige becomes a WEIGHT, never a FILTER. This is what keeps ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist and the 1974 reading-automaticity paper out of the corpus.

### STAGE 3 — VOTE. New: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/canon_vote.py`
Fetch `reference[]` for every SELECTED seed. Count DOIs. Emit ranked anchors + the concentration diagnostic + the escalation ladder from section 4. **These are the anchors. Nobody typed them.**

### STAGE 4 — EXPAND
- **Backward (primary):** Crossref `reference[]` from the derived anchors, depth 2. Reuses `journal_corpus_build.py`'s working machinery — only its anchor SOURCE changes, from a hand list to the vote.
- **Forward (fill only):** COCI at `api.opencitations.net/index/v1/citations/{doi}` (follow the 301; streaming retry for IncompleteRead), fired ONLY at anchors whose matrix cells are still EMPTY. 819 of JEP-2019's 1,334 citers are 2024+ — a precision instrument aimed at a named hole, not a foundation.
- DELETE `scripts/deep_fetch.py:78-88` (S2 fallbacks: 404 today; they silently return nothing while inflating the perceived fetch budget). DELETE all OpenAlex list/filter calls (429). KEEP OpenAlex single-entity (200) purely for abstract recovery where Crossref has none.

### STAGE 5 — SELECT AGAIN. Same gate, same `Scope`. One admission authority for the whole system.

### STAGE 6 — WEIGHT (ranks WITHIN scope; never admits)
`co_citation_votes` x `topic_relevance_score` x `venue_tier` x `recency` x `groundability`. The vote is a **better canonicity signal than raw `is-referenced-by-count`** because it is *relevance-conditioned*: it counts citations **from the on-topic frontier**, not from all of science. ResNet has ~200k citations and **zero votes here** — which is exactly why it is in our corpus today and would not be under this design.

### STAGE 7 — DEDUP / CONSOLIDATE
DOI-canonical + title-similarity clustering to merge WP/journal version pairs into ONE work carrying the journal identity and the WP text (Sol's working-paper text lane, kept). Fix the lying full-text detector in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_fetch.py` (operator FIX 7.3: it accepted a 548-word Oxford landing page reading "CONTACT Name Email... We welcome feedback" as FULLTEXT for Frey & Osborne): require >=1500 words AND title-in-body AND no landing-page chrome.

### STAGE 8 — STOP. **When the matrix stops filling, not when a counter hits 100.**
Halt when (a) every cell is FILLED or DECLARED_GAP, AND (b) two consecutive expansion rounds add <3 newly-filled cells, AND (c) concentration >= threshold. Empty cells at halt are not failures — they are the **future-research agenda**, measured rather than asserted. Sol had this idea; it now runs on a matrix nobody typed.

## 8. WHAT RETRIEVAL OWES THE COMPOSER
Retrieval does not score. It removes the ceiling. It hands the composer four things it cannot otherwise have: **2024+ journal evidence** (we have zero; cellcog leans on it 164 times); a **CONTRADICTION INDEX** (Fable's idea — but a contradiction index over 70 mostly-off-topic works is noise, while one over a matrix-saturated on-topic corpus is the raw material for cellcog's highest-value section); **MEASURED GAPS** (the empty cells); and an **AUDITABLE PROVENANCE STORY** that makes the Scope-and-Methods section true rather than asserted.</design>

## WALKTHROUGH 2 — "What caused the 2008 financial crisis" (HISTORICAL)

**SCOPE (derived):** recency = **~2010-01-01, NOT 2024.** This is the case that kills a hardcoded date floor: the 2008 literature peaked 2009-2015, and a 2024+ filter returns retrospectives and pedagogy, not primary research. The window is a derived property of the question's temporal locus.

**venue_class (derived):** journals **plus NBER/Fed/IMF working papers**, which are PRIMARY sources here. **A hardcoded "no working papers" rule — which Sol proposes, and which task 72's prompt genuinely requires — would be a scholarly error on this question.** One rule cannot be right for both. Only derivation is.

**FRONTIER -> VOTE:** I ran this twice, and **the first run FAILED** — concentration 13%, top anchors a *Who's Who* entry and an obituary for Geoffrey Milne (1898-1942). **This is the single most important result in my report, because it shows the system has a failure mode and can NAME it at runtime.** With SELECT applied to the seeds before the vote, concentration rose to **28%** and the canon appeared: Mian & Sufi QJE 2009 (x14), Keys-Mukherjee-Seru-Vig QJE 2010 (x11), Demyanyk & Van Hemert RFS (x8), Piskorski-Seru-Vig JFE (x7), Mayer-Pence-Sherlund JEP (x6). <faithfulness_position>## THE LINE
**FABRICATION = putting words in a source's mouth. SCHOLARSHIP = our own reasoning, marked as such.** The moat is NOT "every sentence must be span-grounded" — it is "no sentence may put words in a source's mouth." I adopt the operator's formulation exactly, and Fable's three refinements without amendment, because Fable's decode MEASURED that 71% of the #1 system's sentences carry no citation and that it stays honest by LABELLING, not by refusing to think.

## WHAT WE KEEP (this is what protects a research product people TRUST)
1. **No number, entity, study, or attribution that is not in an admitted extractive span.** Non-negotiable. A 0.60 obtained by fabricating is a 0.00.
2. **The mechanism pool is the union of the SENTENCE'S OWN cited cards** — never the corpus. Fable proved a corpus-wide pool degenerates the substring test at `synthesis_contract.py:197-205` into "does this sentence contain a word the field uses", and **flips our own ATTACK #1 from REJECT to PASS.** ATTACK #1 stays REJECTED as a CI canary on every commit. bodhi never needed the widening: its bridge sentence already cites the mechanism's paper.
3. **Hedge + tag alone is COSTUME, never a license.** An analytical synthesis must be a CONDITIONAL over >=2 ADMITTED premises carrying `premise_ids`, so the no-new-number and no-new-entity gates STILL RUN underneath it.
4. **Gap claims are CORPUS-SCOPED.** cellcog writes "No peer-reviewed study has yet measured X" — a false universal it cannot know. We write "Within the 137 journal articles retrieved for this review, none measures X." True by construction; identical judge-visible payoff.
5. **Every mechanism carries its OWN verbatim span** (kills `cellcog_composer.py:167`).
6. **`validate()` ON the critical path**, with a poisoned-card CI test. This bug has shipped twice. Never again.

## WHAT WE DROP (costs score, protects nothing)
1. **The `entailment_judge.py:588` NEUTRAL clause** ("delete any sentence introducing a fact, entity, mechanism or specificity NOT present in the SPAN"). It deletes every cross-source inference BY CONSTRUCTION, on the heaviest-weighted dimension, and it is why 98% of our sentences are bare facts. Replaced by the typed contract — not by nothing.
2. **The single-span union entailment gate.** It kills bodhi's winning sentence, whose two components are each fully grounded in the two papers it cites.
3. **The blanket working-paper reject as a CORPUS INPUT rule.** Keep the ban on CITING non-journals when the prompt demands journals (task 72 does, w=0.15); drop it for TEXT SUBSTRATE. Unpaywall cannot reach the econ canon (ALM 2003 `is_oa=false`; AER 2018's OA URL is a 403 Cloudflare wall), but NBER/IZA carry the same paper. Cite the journal version; span-check against the WP text; **never quote a number from a WP PDF** (version drift is real). Note that for the 2008 question the DERIVED venue class admits working papers as citable — and that is correct.
4. **The hard-coded abstract at `cellcog_composer.py:400-414`**, which asserts "draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" regardless of what the corpus actually contains. **That is a fabricated compliance claim aimed squarely at the instruction-following grader — fabrication with a score motive, and the worst single thing in our codebase.** The abstract is derived from admitted cards; the Scope section may state only what a corpus audit proves at compose time.

## THE NEW RULE MY OWN DESIGN FORCES (nobody has this, and I introduced the risk)
The co-citation vote opens a fabrication channel that did not previously exist: **it can promote a paper we have never read.** A derived anchor is a DOI plus a vote count — that is ALL it is. If "Autor, Levy and Murnane (2003), in the *QJE*, showed that..." reaches prose on the strength of vote rank alone, we have asserted a finding we never saw. That is fabrication arrived at through a clever mechanism, which makes it worse, not better.

**RULE: VOTE RANK LICENSES ATTENTION, NEVER ASSERTION.** An anchor must pass fetch -> select -> card-extraction with a verbatim span before one word about it reaches the page. Vote rank is a retrieval priority. It is never evidence.

**Corollary — canonicity claims are corpus-scoped too.** We may not write "the seminal study in this field." We write "the most frequently cited work among the 214 reference lists surveyed for this review" — **measured, true by construction, and something no other system on the board can say.**

## WHERE RETRIEVAL PROVENANCE BECOMES SCORE
The RACE cleaner deletes markers and bibliographies; `leaderboard_gpt55.csv` shows `citation_accuracy` and `effective_citations` as "-" for all ten systems. **The judge cannot see our provenance — except in prose.** cellcog's §1.2 "Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" narrates its selection criteria and pre-empts the working-paper objection in prose; its instruction-following is 0.5530 against our 0.4409. A DERIVED scope makes that section true and specific: we can state the derived recency window, the query set, the vote, the concentration, the matrix saturation, and the declared gaps. **Corpus-scoped honesty and instruction-following score are the same sentence.** That is the one place where doing the scholarship properly and scoring well are not in tension — and it is the whole reason the faithfulness line is worth defending rather than merely tolerating.</faithfulness_position>

It is dependent on a Crossref date filter returning 278,427 journal articles in 1.5s — verified today.", "The hand-read 8-dimension x 10-industry coverage matrix, the hand-listed industries, the 10-section outline, and the topic regex — all generated at runtime by RACE's own criteria generator (third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py), which is handed only the task text.", "The ASSUMPTION that the dimension weights are fixed at INSIGHT .32 / COMPREH .29 / INSTR .25 / READ .14. generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt GENERATES them per-task from the prompt. Read them at runtime; do not hardcode where to spend. Both plans hardcode this.", "Sol's 4IR SPINE (a paragraph per H2 comparing AI to mechanization/electrification/computerization). Fable's decode refutes it from the #1 artifact: cellcog mentions prior industrial revolutions once and explicitly demotes the 4IR frame. The system that DOES build the four-revolution comparison is WhaleCloud, at 0.5396.", "Sol's Format-D attribution ('Writing in the QJE in 2003, Autor, Levy and Murnane show...'). Measured: nobody on the board writes this way; cellcog's workhorse form is 'Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal Name*, find that...' (73 instances).", "'recency-bonus for >=2023' as a WEIGHT. Recency is a DERIVED HARD ADMISSION GATE. A weight cannot keep 2019 papers out of a corpus that needs 2025 ones.", "Venue prestige as a FILTER. It admitted ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist and a 1974 Cognitive Psychology paper on reading automaticity, while rejecting on-topic OA work. Prestige is a WEIGHT, never a FILTER.", "Fable's SSRN/NBER container-title blocklist as the venue mechanism. Measured: SSRN Electronic Journal carries ISSN 1556-5068 and passes filter=type:journal-article (16 of the top 100 hits), so an ISSN allowlist does not catch it — and a name blocklist would wrongly exclude NBER from the 2008-crisis question, where it is a primary source.", "The Semantic Scholar fallbacks at scripts/deep_fetch.py:78-88 (404 today; they silently return nothing while inflating the perceived fetch budget) and ALL OpenAlex list/filter endpoints (hard 429). Keep OpenAlex single-entity (200 OK) for abstract recovery only.", "The entailment_judge.py:588 NEUTRAL clause and the single-span union entailment gate. They delete every cross-source inference by construction, on the heaviest-weighted dimension, and they kill bodhi's winning sentence. Replaced by the typed contract — not by nothing.", "The hard-coded abstract at cellcog_composer.py:400-414, which asserts 'draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion' regardless of the corpus. A fabricated compliance claim aimed at the instruction-following grader.", "The 15-sources-per-section floor. Unsatisfiable on a real corpus, so it is silently padded — it is what routed 104 off-topic medical papers into the Introduction.", "The docstring lemma at scripts/journal_corpus_build.py:8-14 ('WHY NOT KEYWORD SEARCH... WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS: we already hold those anchors'). It is the overfit, written down, and load-bearing."]</what_to_drop>
<expected_gain>**I will not claim the composer's points as mine.** Retrieval does not score; it removes the ceiling that caps what the composer can score. Here is the honest split.

**What retrieval OWNS and can be held to:**
- **Recency: 0 -> 40+ post-2023 journal works.** Our corpus has ZERO papers after 2023. cellcog mentions 2023/2024/2025 **164 times**; bodhi 11; cellcog is #1. Two criteria pay for exactly this ("current, high-quality academic literature"; "magnitude, SPEED, and transformative potential"). This is the largest *measured* gap between us and #1, and it is now a solved engineering problem rather than a research risk.
- **On-topic density: 17/120 -> >90%.** The corpus that holds ResNet and a 1974 reading-psychology paper is replaced by one whose every member passed an LLM relevance judge against the actual question.
- **Corpus size: ~45 on-topic -> 120-160 works, with a DERIVED canon at its core.**
- **A contradiction index and a measured gap list** — the substrate for the adjudication prose that is cellcog's highest-scoring section, and which we currently cannot write at all, because our corpus is too thin and too off-topic to contain two papers that genuinely disagree about the same thing.

**Numbers — stated as what they are: estimates from the board's spread, not measured effects.**
- COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 -> 0.55-0.58. Retrieval is the direct cause ("Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized"; "Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis" via matrix saturation). **+0.10 x 0.29 = +0.029.** The estimate I am most confident in.
- INSIGHT 0.4238 -> 0.54-0.58. Retrieval supplies the substrate; the COMPOSER converts it. I claim perhaps half of this and will not pretend otherwise. **+0.12 x 0.32 = +0.038**, of which retrieval is a *necessary condition* for maybe +0.02.
- INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.4409 -> 0.55-0.57. The derived venue gate makes "only cites high-quality journal articles" (w=0.15) TRUE rather than asserted, and a truthful, specific Scope-and-Methods section is the one place retrieval provenance is judge-visible. **+0.12 x 0.25 = +0.030.**
- READABILITY 0.3774 -> 0.52-0.54. **Retrieval contributes NOTHING here.** Pure composer mechanics (677w -> ~100w paragraphs, 30+ H3, a summary table, killing the meta-opener). It is also the single highest-confidence move in the whole project. **+0.15 x 0.14 = +0.021.**

**SUM: 0.4382 + 0.029 + 0.038 + 0.030 + 0.021 = ~0.556 single compose.** Best-of-3 composes adds roughly +0.008. **Point estimate ~0.564.**

**Against a target of ~0.556 +/- 0.019 — which is a REGRESSION ESTIMATE, not a measured number, because cellcog has no task-72 row — that is a win by less than one sigma. P(best-of-3 > 0.5578) ~ 60-65%.** I land where Sol landed, and I will not dress it up better than he did. **But I get there with his largest named risk (35%, forward traversal, "an infrastructure fix, not a writing fix") STRUCTURALLY REMOVED rather than mitigated — and with a system that also answers the other 99 tasks.**

**The one number that would change my estimate:** the cellcog-ablation experiment (design §6). If stripping cellcog's 2024+ citations costs it less than ~0.01, my recency thesis is worth far less than I have booked — and I want that number BEFORE we build, not after.</expected_gain>
<honest_risks>["THE LOAD-BEARING UNKNOWN IS STILL UNKNOWN, AND RETRIEVAL CANNOT ANSWER IT. We have proven the judge can SEE our scholarship; we have not proven it PAYS for it. A better corpus the judge does not price is worth zero. This is precisely why the cellcog-ablation experiment must run FIRST — it is the only proposal on the table that prices a feature before we build the machine to produce it.", "REFERENCE-LIST DEPOSIT RATE IS A HARD FLOOR ON THE VOTE. Measured: only 17 of 40 unfiltered seeds (42%) carry deposited reference lists in Crossref. Adding filter=has-references:true lifts it to ~83% — but that filter SELECTS FOR PUBLISHERS WHO DEPOSIT (Elsevier, Wiley, Springer) and silently biases the derived canon toward them, away from society journals and non-Western publishers who do not. The vote therefore measures 'most-cited BY REF-DEPOSITING PUBLISHERS', not 'most-cited'. I do not know how large this bias is and I have not measured it.", "CO-CITATION IS A POPULARITY MEASURE. It reliably finds the canon; it will systematically MISS the contrarian paper, the recent null, and the heterodox account that make a review insightful rather than merely correct. Mitigations exist (empty matrix cells drive targeted search; the contradiction index hunts disagreement) but the vote's own gradient points at CONSENSUS — the direction AWAY from the heaviest-weighted dimension. This is a real tension in my design and I have fenced it, not resolved it.", "THE CONCENTRATION THRESHOLD (~0.25) IS CALIBRATED ON n=4 RUNS I DID TONIGHT (59% / 29% / 28% / 13%). It is a heuristic with a sample size of four, not a law. There is certainly a field with a genuinely diffuse citation structure where it fires a false alarm, and it will need recalibration across more domains.", "MY 2008 FIX CHANGED TWO THINGS AT ONCE. Concentration went 13% -> 28% from SELECT-gating the seeds AND rewriting the query with field vocabulary ('subprime mortgage securitization leverage'). I did not ablate them separately. I attribute most of the gain to SELECT, but I have NOT proven that — and the query-vocabulary half depends on an LLM guessing a field's terms correctly, which is a weaker link than I would like.", "THE VOTE OPENS A FABRICATION CHANNEL THAT DID NOT EXIST BEFORE. A derived anchor is a DOI and a vote count — a paper we have never read. If vote rank alone licenses prose ('the seminal study in this field shows...'), we assert a finding we never saw, via a clever mechanism, which makes it worse and not better. Gated by 'vote rank licenses attention, never assertion' — but this is a NEW risk that I introduced, and it must be tested adversarially, not assumed closed.", "COVERAGE-MATRIX SATURATION MAY BE UNREACHABLE AND WE WILL NOT KNOW UNTIL WE RUN IT. Sol wants >=80% of 80 cells with >=2 numbered studies each — that is 128+ well-grounded studies. If the literature simply does not contain two numbered journal studies of AI's wage effects in agriculture, that cell can only ever be a DECLARED_GAP: honest, and worth something, but not the coverage the arithmetic booked.", "CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED MAY DOMINATE EVERY LEVER HERE, IN EITHER DIRECTION. The judge scores all ~25 criteria in ONE comparative call and writes the comparative analysis BEFORE the numbers. If all four dimensions land within 0.01 of each other and all are ~0.01-0.02 short, no retrieval fix reaches it (Sol's X3, and he is right to name it) — it would mean sentence-level prose quality is the binding constraint and the entire retrieval investment is necessary but not sufficient.", "EVEN IF EVERYTHING WORKS, THE MARGIN IS UNDER ONE SIGMA. Point estimate ~0.564 against a target of ~0.556 +/- 0.019 that IS NOT A MEASURED NUMBER — cellcog has no task-72 row; it is a regression estimate whose residual SD (0.0094) is exactly our smallest resolvable effect. We may beat the estimate and lose to the truth. Nothing in my design fixes that, and no plan submitted tonight can."]</honest_risks>
</invoke>
