input.thesis:
The canon is not searched for and not hand-named — it is the FIXED POINT OF CO-CITATION FROM THE FRONTIER. Keyword search fails on canonical papers because their titles use their era's vocabulary (Autor-Levy-Murnane 2003 contains neither "AI" nor "labor market"), but it succeeds on RECENT papers precisely because their titles use today's vocabulary — so we date-filter-search the frontier, harvest the frontier's reference lists, and let them VOTE; the winners of that vote ARE the canon, derived at runtime, for any field. This inverts the one broken graph edge (forward traversal, dead on this box) into the one that works (backward references, 200 OK), retiring Sol's 35% risk and Fable's 100%-COCI dependency in a single move — and every remaining constant (scope, matrix, outline, stopping rule) is generated from the prompt by the judge's own criteria generator.</thesis>
<design">PLACEHOLDER-IGNORE</design>
<design>## THE ONE-LINE ARCHITECTURE

DERIVE SCOPE -> SEARCH FRONTIER (date-filtered) -> SELECT (LLM, vs the actual question) -> VOTE (co-citation) -> EXPAND (backward refs) -> SELECT -> WEIGHT -> DEDUP -> CONSOLIDATE -> STOP (matrix saturation).

The stage that is NEW is VOTE. The stage that is in the wrong PLACE in both existing plans is SELECT.

## 1. THE INVERSION — the root fix, not a tweak

/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py:8-14 is a docstring that states a true observation, draws a false conclusion, and hardcodes its way around it:

"WHY NOT KEYWORD SEARCH: tried and rejected, with evidence... Crossref sorted by relevance: returns papers with the keywords IN THE TITLE, which are the LOW-QUALITY ones (0-25 citations, near-predatory venues). The canonical papers do not have 'artificial intelligence labor market employment' verbatim in their titles. WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS: ... We already hold those anchors (Frey & Osborne, Autor, Acemoglu & Restrepo, Eloundou, Noy & Zhang, Felten)."

Every observation there is TRUE. The conclusion is BACKWARDS. Keyword search is bad at finding the CANON and good at finding the FRONTIER — for one and the same reason: TITLE VOCABULARY TRACKS PUBLICATION DATE. The docstring found the frontier and threw it away because it was hunting the canon. Sol's STEP 1 ("15 canonical anchors, hand-named, not searched") and Fable's "1-2 hand-verified canonical anchors PER MATRIX CELL" are the same surrender, and they are the overfit the operator caught (FIX 4).

The frontier is not junk to discard. THE FRONTIER IS THE BALLOT BOX. Recent papers cite the canon. So:
1. Keyword-search WITH A DATE FILTER -> recent papers (they match, because they are written in your vocabulary).
2. Pull each one's reference list from Crossref (works, 200 OK).
3. Count DOIs. The distribution is not flat.
4. The top of that distribution IS the canon.

### I RAN THIS TONIGHT. IT IS NOT A PROPOSAL.
Query: "artificial intelligence labor market employment wages displacement", filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01, rows=40. 17 seeds carried deposited reference lists; 296 distinct cited DOIs harvested. The co-citation vote, verbatim:

  x5  10.1016/j.techfore.2016.08.019  Frey & Osborne, TFSC 2017
  x5  10.1162/003355303322552801      Autor, Levy & Murnane, QJE 2003  <-- the paper "keyword search PROVABLY cannot find"
  x4  10.1002/smj.3286                Felten, Raj & Seamans, SMJ
  x3  10.1257/aer.103.5.1553          Autor & Dorn, AER 2013 (polarization)
  x3  10.1257/jep.33.2.3              Acemoglu & Restrepo, JEP 2019
  x3  10.1257/pandp.20181019          Brynjolfsson, Mitchell & Rock   <-- bodhi's source [1]
  x3  10.1086/705716                  Acemoglu & Restrepo, JPE 2020
  x3  10.1086/718327                  Acemoglu & Restrepo             <-- bodhi's source [3]
  x2  10.1126/science.adj0998         Eloundou et al., Science 2024
  x2  10.1257/aer.20160696            Acemoglu & Restrepo, AER (Race between Man and Machine)
  x2  10.1257/jep.29.3.3              Autor, JEP 2015
  x1  10.1257/jel.40.1.7              Acemoglu, JEL

That is ELEVEN OF SOL'S FIFTEEN HAND-NAMED ANCHORS, DERIVED IN ~40 SECONDS FROM NOTHING BUT THE TASK PROMPT — including ALM 2003, the paper SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:98-105 and OPERATOR FIX 4 both cite as proof that keyword search cannot find the canon. It cannot. Co-citation from the frontier can. It also independently rediscovered both sources behind bodhi's winning sentence. The anchor list is now DERIVED.

## 2. RECENCY COMES FROM SEARCH, NOT TRAVERSAL — measured

Operator FIX 1 re-verified by me: GET api.crossref.org/works?query.bibliographic=generative+AI+labor+market+employment&filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01 -> HTTP 200 in 1.56s, total-results 278,427.

Our corpus has ZERO works after 2023. cellcog mentions 2023/2024/2025 164 times; bodhi 11; cellcog is #1. That gap — the largest measured deficit in the brief — is closed by a query string. Not by infrastructure. Not by a proxy. By a query string.

BOTH PLANS GOT THE DIRECTION WRONG, NOT THE EFFORT. They needed 2003-anchors -> 2024-papers, i.e. the CANON->RECENT edge, i.e. forward traversal, i.e. the dead one. Channel status measured by me tonight:
- Crossref /works/{doi} reference[] (BACKWARD): 200 OK. ALM 2003 -> 80/80 refs returned. is-referenced-by-count 4743.
- Crossref date-filtered search: 200 OK, 278k results, 1.5s.
- OpenAlex single-entity /works/doi:{doi}: 200 OK.
- OpenAlex list/filter (?filter=cites:): 429 Rate limit exceeded.
- Semantic Scholar graph API: 404.
- OpenCitations COCI (FORWARD): 200 — BUT ONLY AFTER A 301. Fable's URL is stale: opencitations.net/index/coci/api/v1 redirects to api.opencitations.net/index/v1. Follow it and you get 1,334 citing DOIs for JEP 2019, 819 of them 2024+. The body also arrives truncated on a plain read (Fable's IncompleteRead) and needs a streaming retry.

We do not need the dead edge. We start at the frontier and walk BACKWARD — the direction that returns 200. SOL'S GATE 0 (a BLOCKING preflight demanding "20 consecutive HTTP 200 forward-citation lookups", failing which "post-2023 coverage is at risk" and the kill rule arms) IS DELETED AS A BLOCKING GATE. COCI forward survives, demoted to a FILL tool aimed at specific empty matrix cells, used AFTER we know which cells are empty. Sol priced forward-traversal failure at "subtract 0.015-0.020... a bodhi-class outcome", at 35%. That risk is not mitigated. It is STRUCTURALLY GONE.

## 3. SELECT IS THE BROKEN STAGE — and I proved it by breaking my own pipeline

I did not take operator FIX 2 on faith. I tested the vote on two foreign domains, and the design nearly died.

CLINICAL — "CAR-T therapy versus bispecific antibodies relapsed refractory multiple myeloma", from 2024-01-01. Same code. Zero changes:
  x10 10.1038/s41591-023-02528-9     Elranatamab in RRMM (MagnetisMM-3), Nature Medicine
  x9  10.1056/nejmoa2203478          Teclistamab in RRMM (MajesTEC-1), NEJM
  x9  10.1056/nejmoa2204591          Talquetamab, GPRC5D bispecific (MonumenTAL-1), NEJM
  x8  10.1056/nejmoa2024850          Idecabtagene vicleucel (KarMMa), NEJM
  x7  10.1016/s0140-6736(21)00933-8  Ciltacabtagene autoleucel (CARTITUDE-1), Lancet
  x5  10.1038/s41591-023-02491-5     Mechanisms of antigen escape from BCMA/GPRC5D
  x5  10.1126/scitranslmed.aau7746   GPRC5D is a target for the immunotherapy of myeloma
Precisely the pivotal trials plus the mechanism papers. A myeloma specialist would name that exact list. Nothing in the code knows what myeloma is.

HISTORICAL — "causes of the 2008 global financial crisis", from 2015-01-01. IT FAILED:
  x2  10.1093/ww/9780199540884.013.257752  Llewellyn of Steep, Baron  <-- a WHO'S WHO ENTRY
  x2  10.5040/9781474230636.0017           Geoffrey Milne 1898-1942   <-- AN OBITUARY
  x2  10.4337/9781800370890.00012          Professor Tim Congdon CBE
  x2  10.3167/ip.2004.200112               The Collapse of Parmalat
  x2  10.59534/jcss.1528668                ...the Syrian Crisis...
Garbage. And the failure HAS A NUMBER: top anchor got 2 votes from 15 ref-bearing seeds = 13% concentration, against 59% (myeloma) and 29% (AI/labour).

The bug was in MY OWN PROBE, and it was exactly operator FIX 2: I HARVESTED REFERENCE LISTS FROM UNSELECTED SEEDS. Books, encyclopedias and obituaries voted. So I moved SELECT before the vote — a structural gate on the seeds (real ISSN-bearing journal container, deposited reference list) plus a query carrying the field's vocabulary — and re-ran with nothing else changed.

2008 crisis, SELECT-gated seeds (50 of 60). Concentration 13% -> 28%:
  x14 10.1162/qjec.2009.124.4.1449  Mian & Sufi, QJE 2009 — Consequences of Mortgage Credit Expansion
  x11 10.1162/qjec.2010.125.1.307   Keys, Mukherjee, Seru & Vig, QJE 2010 — Did Securitization Lead to Lax Screening?
  x8  10.1093/rfs/hhp033            Demyanyk & Van Hemert, RFS — Understanding the Subprime Mortgage Crisis
  x7  10.1016/j.jfineco.2010.04.003 Piskorski, Seru & Vig, JFE
  x6  10.1257/jep.23.1.27           Mayer, Pence & Sherlund, JEP — The Rise in Mortgage Defaults

The canon of the 2008 literature, derived. SELECT-BEFORE-EXPAND IS NOT A HYGIENE STEP; IT IS THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN MIAN-SUFI AND AN OBITUARY. Sol runs SELECT after expansion; Fable runs it as stage (e) of eight, after the graph is already built. Both let the junk vote.

## 4. THE SELF-DIAGNOSTIC — what makes this a system and not a demo

That concentration number is not a debugging aid I used once. It is a RUNTIME SIGNAL, computable with no ground truth, that tells the system whether its own retrieval worked.

concentration = (votes for top anchor) / (number of selected, ref-bearing seeds)

Measured tonight over four runs: 59% (myeloma), 29% (AI/labour), 28% (2008 selected), 13% (2008 unselected — the failure). Coherent seed set -> a small set of works is co-cited by a large fraction of the frontier. Junk seed set -> votes scatter and the max collapses to 1-2.

It drives an ESCALATION LADDER instead of a silent bad corpus:
- concentration >= ~0.25 -> the field has a canon and we found it. EXPAND.
- concentration < ~0.25 -> DO NOT PROCEED. Reformulate: widen the date window (the 2008 literature peaks 2010-2015, not 2024+ — the window is derived from the question's temporal locus, not a constant); regenerate queries from the FIELD's vocabulary rather than the USER's phrasing; drop ISSN-less seeds; re-vote.
- still low after N reformulations -> the field genuinely has no citation canon (true for some policy/industry questions). The system SAYS SO in the Scope section and falls back to breadth-first date-filtered search with the LLM SELECT gate carrying the whole load. An honest degraded mode, declared in prose.

Neither plan has a retrieval stage that can tell you it failed. Both would have shipped the obituary and blamed the composer.

## 5. THE FULL CHAIN, WITH FILE:LINE

STAGE 0 — DERIVE SCOPE. New: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/scope.py
Input: the task prompt, and nothing else. Output a Scope object, every field derived by one LLM call: topic; recency_window (task 72 -> 2024-01-01; "2008 crisis" -> ~2010-01-01, the literature's locus, not today) as a HARD ADMISSION GATE per operator FIX 3, not Sol's "recency-bonus for >=2023" (a weight cannot keep 2019 papers out of a corpus that needs 2025 ones); venue_class (task 72's prompt literally says "Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles" -> peer_reviewed_journal_only — READ FROM THE PROMPT, not typed by an engineer); language; and query_seeds (8-15 strings spanning the field's vocabulary).

WHY venue_class MUST BE DERIVED — I measured the trap. In the top 100 Crossref results for our own query under filter=type:journal-article:
  16  SSRN Electronic Journal   <-- A PREPRINT SERVER, PASSING A JOURNAL-ARTICLE FILTER
   3  Selected Issues Papers
   2  IMF Working Papers
   2  IZA World of Labor
SSRN Electronic Journal CARRIES ISSN 1556-5068. So Fable's "ISSN allowlist" does not save you, and a hardcoded blocklist of {SSRN, NBER} is just more overfit. The general test is structural (volume + issue + pages + non-repository DOI prefix + real container ISSN) COMBINED with the derived venue_class. And here is the point that settles the whole argument: THE SAME RULE THAT MUST EXCLUDE NBER FOR TASK 72 MUST INCLUDE IT FOR "WHAT CAUSED THE 2008 FINANCIAL CRISIS", where NBER and Fed research are PRIMARY sources and excluding them is a scholarly error. A hardcoded venue rule is wrong on one of those two questions no matter how you set it. Only derivation is right on both.

STAGE 0b — DERIVE THE COVERAGE MATRIX. New: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/matrix.py
The operator's keystone (FIX 4). I verified the mechanism at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py: it holds four generators — generate_eval_criteria_prompt_comp / _insight / _instruction / _readability — plus generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt. EVERY ONE IS .format(task_prompt=...): handed the task text and NOTHING ELSE. Each returns JSON {criterion, explanation, weight} summing to 1.0. So we run the grader's own generator on the same prompt and get the same ~25 criteria the judge will grade us against; the matrix axes are read from THOSE criteria, not off task 72's rubric by hand as Sol does ("Rows = the restructuring dimensions the rubric names VERBATIM... Columns = 10 industries").

A BONUS NEITHER DESIGNER NOTICED: generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt PROVES THE DIMENSION WEIGHTS ARE ALSO GENERATED PER-TASK. INSIGHT .32 / COMPREH .29 / INSTR .25 / READ .14 is not a property of RACE — it is task 72's generated weight vector. The file's own example_2 shows COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.40 / INSIGHT 0.15 for a data-overview task. A SYSTEM THAT HARDCODES "INSIGHT IS THE HEAVIEST WEIGHT, SPEND THERE" IS OVERFIT TO TASK 72 IN THE ONE PLACE NOBODY WAS LOOKING. We generate the weights at runtime and let them tell us where to spend. Two lines of code; large generality payoff.
Cell state: EMPTY | THIN (1 card) | FILLED (>=2 independent works, each with a number and a stated design) | DECLARED_GAP (corpus-scoped).

STAGE 1 — SEARCH THE FRONTIER. New: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/frontier.py
Crossref query.bibliographic x Scope.query_seeds x filter=type:journal-article,has-references:true,from-pub-date:{recency_window}. Adding has-references:true is MEASURED to lift the ref-bearing rate from 17/40 (42%) to ~50/60 (83%) — it triples the ballot box for free. Polite pool with mailto.

STAGE 2 — SELECT. Rewire, don't rewrite: /home/polaris/wt/compose/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/topic_relevance_gate.py
It already exists and is already the right shape: _build_batch_prompt(research_question, ...) at :261 batches candidates to an LLM judge against the actual question. ITS OWN DOCSTRING, LINE 8, ALREADY SAYS THE THING THE OPERATOR HAD TO REDISCOVER: "tier system rates CREDIBILITY, not RELEVANCE, so a credible-but-irrelevant [source passes]". The codebase already knows. It sits behind topic_gate_enabled() (:57) and is NOT ON THE CRITICAL PATH — the identical failure pattern as validate(). We wire it on, hand it Scope, and it becomes the ONLY admission authority. Prestige becomes a WEIGHT, never a FILTER. This is what keeps ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist and the 1974 reading-automaticity paper out.

STAGE 3 — VOTE. New: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/canon_vote.py
Fetch reference[] for every SELECTED seed. Count DOIs. Emit ranked anchors + the concentration diagnostic + the escalation ladder. These are the anchors. Nobody typed them.

STAGE 4 — EXPAND.
Backward (primary): Crossref reference[] from the derived anchors, depth 2. Reuses journal_corpus_build.py's working machinery; only its anchor SOURCE changes, from a hand list to the vote.
Forward (fill only): COCI at api.opencitations.net/index/v1/citations/{doi} (follow the 301; streaming retry for IncompleteRead), fired ONLY at anchors whose matrix cells are still EMPTY. 819 of JEP-2019's 1,334 citers are 2024+ — a precision instrument aimed at a named hole, not a foundation.
DELETE scripts/deep_fetch.py:78-88 (S2 fallbacks: 404 today; they silently return nothing while inflating the perceived fetch budget). DELETE all OpenAlex list/filter calls (429). KEEP OpenAlex single-entity (200) purely for abstract recovery where Crossref has none.

STAGE 5 — SELECT AGAIN. Same gate, same Scope. One admission authority for the whole system.

STAGE 6 — WEIGHT (ranks WITHIN scope; never admits).
co_citation_votes x topic_relevance_score x venue_tier x recency x groundability. The vote is a BETTER canonicity signal than raw is-referenced-by-count because it is RELEVANCE-CONDITIONED: it counts citations FROM THE ON-TOPIC FRONTIER, not from all of science. ResNet has ~200k citations and ZERO votes here — which is exactly why it is in our corpus today and would not be under this design.

STAGE 7 — DEDUP / CONSOLIDATE. DOI-canonical + title-similarity clustering to merge WP/journal version pairs into ONE work carrying the journal identity and the WP text (Sol's working-paper text lane, kept). Fix the lying full-text detector in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_fetch.py (operator FIX 7.3: it accepted a 548-word Oxford landing page reading "CONTACT Name Email... We welcome feedback" as FULLTEXT for Frey & Osborne): require >=1500 words AND title-in-body AND no landing-page chrome.

STAGE 8 — STOP. When the matrix stops filling, not when a counter hits 100. Halt when (a) every cell is FILLED or DECLARED_GAP, (b) two consecutive expansion rounds add <3 newly-filled cells, and (c) concentration >= threshold. Empty cells at halt are not failures — they are the FUTURE-RESEARCH AGENDA, measured rather than asserted. Sol had this idea; it now runs on a matrix nobody typed.

## 6. THE CHEAPEST VISIBILITY-TO-VALUE EXPERIMENT (operator FIX 6) — RUN BEFORE BUILDING ANY OF THE ABOVE

Fable named the load-bearing unknown: we have proven the judge can SEE our scholarship; we have not proven it PAYS. Both plans answer it by building everything and composing once. That is a three-week question.

WE CAN ANSWER IT IN ONE AFTERNOON FOR ~20 JUDGE CALLS AND ZERO BUILD, BECAUSE WE ALREADY HOLD THE #1 ARTIFACT. Take cellcog's task-72 report (/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/cellcog-max.jsonl, id=72) and ablate a document that already scores at the top:
  A: cellcog verbatim (control)
  B: every 2024+ citation swapped for a pre-2023 one -> PRICES RECENCY (the 164-vs-11 gap; my entire angle)
  C: the 13 epistemic tags stripped -> PRICES THE TAG TAXONOMY
  D: the 8 adjudication syntheses flattened into plain reporting -> PRICES ADJUDICATION (the 0.32-weight bet)
Score each k=5 (SD 0.0074; paired resolution +0.0094). This measures THE JUDGE'S PRICE FOR EACH FEATURE, on a document whose score is already anchored, BEFORE we spend an hour building the machine that produces them. If the judge does not pay for recency, my retrieval angle is worth far less than I have booked, and I want to know on day one, not day twenty. Nobody proposed pricing a feature on someone else's artifact. It is nearly free.

## 7. THE THREE CODE BUGS — re-verified, all real
1. validate() IS IMPORTED AND NEVER CALLED. grep -n "validate(" /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py -> line 49 "from synthesis_contract import Premise, Synthesis, validate, OPERATIONS" and ZERO call sites in the file. The only thing between LLM prose and the page is _clean(), a regex strip, which the composer then PRINTS as "sentences DROPPED by the contract". The safety contract is theatre. Commits 225b323 ("mechanism never operated") and b3acb72 ("the judge fell off the critical path") are this same bug, twice. Fix: structured sentence ledger; every sentence through validate(); a CI test asserting a poisoned card yields zero poisoned sentences on the page.
2. cellcog_composer.py:167 — verified verbatim: 'mechanisms': f.get('mechanisms') or [], sits three lines below the span gate at :158-161 that DOES check claim and span against source text. An LLM-invented mechanism string becomes a global citation license (two-hop laundering). Every mechanism carries its own verbatim span or it dies.
3. The full-text detector (journal_corpus_fetch.py) — a lying measurement, fixed at Stage 7.

## 8. WHAT RETRIEVAL OWES THE COMPOSER
Retrieval does not score. It removes the ceiling. It hands the composer four things it cannot otherwise have: 2024+ JOURNAL EVIDENCE (we have zero; cellcog leans on it 164 times); a CONTRADICTION INDEX (Fable's idea — but a contradiction index over 70 mostly-off-topic works is noise, while one over a matrix-saturated on-topic corpus is the raw material for cellcog's highest-value section); MEASURED GAPS (the empty cells); and an AUDITABLE PROVENANCE STORY that makes the Scope-and-Methods section true rather than asserted.</design>

FRONTIER -> VOTE (measured tonight): MagnetisMM-3 (x10), MajesTEC-1 (x9), MonumenTAL-1 (x9), KarMMa (x8), CARTITUDE-1 (x7), the GPRC5D-target paper, the antigen-escape mechanism paper. Concentration 59% — the highest of the three domains, because oncology has an unusually tight canon of pivotal trials. The system proceeds with high confidence, correctly.

COCI forward from KarMMa fills the "sequencing after CAR-T failure" cell with 2025 work. SELECT kills the vision-transformer and radiomics papers any date-filtered oncology query drags in.
CONTRADICTION INDEX: CAR-T's deeper, more durable responses vs bispecifics' off-the-shelf availability and lower early mortality — cards that genuinely diverge, with dimension diffs on population (prior lines) and horizon (durability). WALKTHROUGH 2 — "What caused the 2008 financial crisis" (HISTORICAL)
SCOPE (derived): recency = ~2010-01-01, NOT 2024. This is the case that KILLS a hardcoded date floor: the 2008 literature peaked 2009-2015, and a 2024+ filter returns retrospectives and pedagogy, not primary research. The window is a derived property of the question's temporal locus.
venue_class (derived): journals PLUS NBER/Fed/IMF working papers, which are PRIMARY sources here. A HARDCODED "NO WORKING PAPERS" RULE — WHICH SOL PROPOSES, AND WHICH TASK 72'S PROMPT GENUINELY REQUIRES — WOULD BE A SCHOLARLY ERROR ON THIS QUESTION. One rule cannot be right for both. Only derivation is.
FRONTIER -> VOTE: I ran this twice, and THE FIRST RUN FAILED — concentration 13%, top anchors a Who's Who entry and an obituary for Geoffrey Milne (1898-1942). THIS IS THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT RESULT IN MY REPORT, because it shows the system has a failure mode and can NAME it at runtime. With SELECT applied to the seeds before the vote, concentration rose to 28% and the canon appeared: Mian & Sufi QJE 2009 (x14), Keys-Mukherjee-Seru-Vig QJE 2010 (x11), Demyanyk & Van Hemert RFS (x8), Piskorski-Seru-Vig JFE (x7), Mayer-Pence-Sherlund JEP (x6). That is the literature.
HONEST CAVEAT I WILL NOT HIDE: my selected 2008 run skewed toward the securitization/mortgage sub-literature, because my query said "subprime mortgage securitization leverage". That is the query driving the vote — correct behaviour, but incomplete. In the real system the query seeds come from THE MATRIX (which for this question generates cells for global imbalances, monetary policy, ratings agencies, shadow banking, regulatory capture), so multiple queries produce multiple sub-canons and the matrix — not my hand — enforces balance across the competing causal accounts. That is exactly the mechanism Sol built for industries; I am generalising it to whatever axes the judge's own generator names.

THE PROOF: the three questions share ZERO domain constants. AI/labour derived Autor-Levy-Murnane. Myeloma derived MajesTEC-1. 2008 derived Mian-Sufi. Same code, same six numbers; the only input that changed was the prompt. Sol's and Fable's plans would each need a new hand-built anchor list, a new hand-read matrix and a new topic regex per question — three separate engineering projects. That is the difference between a research system and an expensive way to answer one question.</why_general>
<faithfulness_position>THE LINE: FABRICATION = putting words in a source's mouth. SCHOLARSHIP = our own reasoning, marked as such. The moat is NOT "every sentence must be span-grounded" — it is "no sentence may put words in a source's mouth." I adopt the operator's formulation exactly and Fable's three refinements without amendment, because Fable's decode MEASURED that 71% of the #1 system's sentences carry no citation and that it stays honest by LABELLING, not by refusing to think.

WHAT WE KEEP (this is what protects a research product people TRUST):
1. No number, entity, study, or attribution that is not in an admitted extractive span. Non-negotiable. A 0.60 obtained by fabricating is a 0.00.
2. THE MECHANISM POOL IS THE UNION OF THE SENTENCE'S OWN CITED CARDS — never the corpus. Fable proved a corpus-wide pool degenerates the substring test at synthesis_contract.py:197-205 into "does this sentence contain a word the field uses", and FLIPS OUR OWN ATTACK #1 FROM REJECT TO PASS. ATTACK #1 stays REJECTED as a CI canary on every commit. bodhi never needed the widening: its bridge sentence already cites the mechanism's paper.
3. HEDGE + TAG ALONE IS COSTUME, NEVER A LICENSE. An analytical synthesis must be a CONDITIONAL over >=2 ADMITTED premises carrying premise_ids, so the no-new-number and no-new-entity gates STILL RUN underneath it.
4. GAP CLAIMS ARE CORPUS-SCOPED. cellcog writes "No peer-reviewed study has yet measured X" — a false universal it cannot know. We write "Within the 137 journal articles retrieved for this review, none measures X." True by construction; identical judge-visible payoff.
5. Every mechanism carries its OWN verbatim span (kills cellcog_composer.py:167).
6. validate() ON THE CRITICAL PATH, with a poisoned-card CI test. This bug has shipped twice. Never again.

WHAT WE DROP (costs score, protects nothing):
1. The entailment_judge.py:588 NEUTRAL clause ("delete any sentence introducing a fact, entity, mechanism or specificity NOT present in the SPAN"). It deletes every cross-source inference BY CONSTRUCTION, on the heaviest-weighted dimension, and it is why 98% of our sentences are bare facts. Replaced by the typed contract — not by nothing.
2. The single-span union entailment gate. It kills bodhi's winning sentence, whose two components are each fully grounded in the two papers it cites.
3. The blanket working-paper reject as a CORPUS INPUT rule. Keep the ban on CITING non-journals when the prompt demands journals (task 72 does, w=0.15); drop it for TEXT SUBSTRATE. Unpaywall cannot reach the econ canon (ALM 2003 is_oa=false; AER 2018's OA URL is a 403 Cloudflare wall), but NBER/IZA carry the same paper. Cite the journal version; span-check against the WP text; NEVER quote a number from a WP PDF (version drift is real). Note that for the 2008 question the DERIVED venue class admits working papers as citable — and that is correct.
4. The hard-coded abstract at cellcog_composer.py:400-414, which asserts "draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" regardless of what the corpus actually contains. THAT IS A FABRICATED COMPLIANCE CLAIM AIMED SQUARELY AT THE INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING GRADER — fabrication with a score motive, and the worst single thing in our codebase. The abstract is derived from admitted cards; the Scope section may state only what a corpus audit proves at compose time.

THE NEW RULE MY OWN DESIGN FORCES (nobody has this, and I introduced the risk):
The co-citation vote opens a fabrication channel that did not previously exist: IT CAN PROMOTE A PAPER WE HAVE NEVER READ. A derived anchor is a DOI plus a vote count — that is ALL it is. If "Autor, Levy and Murnane (2003), in the QJE, showed that..." reaches prose on the strength of vote rank alone, we have asserted a finding we never saw. That is fabrication arrived at through a clever mechanism, which makes it worse, not better.
RULE: VOTE RANK LICENSES ATTENTION, NEVER ASSERTION. An anchor must pass fetch -> select -> card-extraction with a verbatim span before one word about it reaches the page. Vote rank is a retrieval priority. It is never evidence.
COROLLARY — CANONICITY CLAIMS ARE CORPUS-SCOPED TOO. We may not write "the seminal study in this field." We write "the most frequently cited work among the 214 reference lists surveyed for this review" — measured, true by construction, and something no other system on the board can say.

WHERE RETRIEVAL PROVENANCE BECOMES SCORE: the RACE cleaner deletes markers and bibliographies; leaderboard_gpt55.csv shows citation_accuracy and effective_citations as "-" for all ten systems. THE JUDGE CANNOT SEE OUR PROVENANCE — EXCEPT IN PROSE. cellcog's §1.2 "Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" narrates its selection criteria and pre-empts the working-paper objection in prose; its instruction-following is 0.5530 against our 0.4409. A DERIVED scope makes that section true and specific: we can state the derived recency window, the query set, the vote, the concentration, the matrix saturation and the declared gaps. CORPUS-SCOPED HONESTY AND INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING SCORE ARE THE SAME SENTENCE. That is the one place where doing the scholarship properly and scoring well are not in tension — and it is the whole reason the faithfulness line is worth defending rather than merely tolerating.</faithfulness_position>

<expected_gain>I WILL NOT CLAIM THE COMPOSER'S POINTS AS MINE. Retrieval does not score; it removes the ceiling that caps what the composer can score. The honest split:

WHAT RETRIEVAL OWNS AND CAN BE HELD TO:
- RECENCY: 0 -> 40+ post-2023 journal works. Our corpus has ZERO papers after 2023. cellcog mentions 2023/2024/2025 164 times; bodhi 11; cellcog is #1. Two criteria pay for exactly this ("current, high-quality academic literature"; "magnitude, SPEED, and transformative potential"). This is the largest MEASURED gap between us and #1, and it is now a solved engineering problem rather than a research risk.
- ON-TOPIC DENSITY: 17/120 -> >90%. The corpus that holds ResNet and a 1974 reading-psychology paper is replaced by one whose every member passed an LLM relevance judge against the actual question.
- CORPUS SIZE: ~45 on-topic -> 120-160 works, with a DERIVED canon at its core.
- A CONTRADICTION INDEX AND A MEASURED GAP LIST — the substrate for the adjudication prose that is cellcog's highest-scoring section, and which we currently cannot write at all because our corpus is too thin and too off-topic to contain two papers that genuinely disagree about the same thing.

NUMBERS — STATED AS WHAT THEY ARE: ESTIMATES FROM THE BOARD'S SPREAD, NOT MEASURED EFFECTS.
- COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.4549 -> 0.55-0.58. Retrieval is the direct cause ("Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized"; "Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis" via matrix saturation). +0.10 x 0.29 = +0.029. The estimate I am most confident in.
- INSIGHT 0.4238 -> 0.54-0.58. Retrieval supplies the substrate; the COMPOSER converts it. I claim perhaps half of this and will not pretend otherwise. +0.12 x 0.32 = +0.038, of which retrieval is a NECESSARY CONDITION for maybe +0.02.
- INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING 0.4409 -> 0.55-0.57. The derived venue gate makes "only cites high-quality journal articles" (w=0.15) TRUE rather than asserted, and a truthful, specific Scope-and-Methods section is the one place retrieval provenance is judge-visible. +0.12 x 0.25 = +0.030.
- READABILITY 0.3774 -> 0.52-0.54. RETRIEVAL CONTRIBUTES NOTHING HERE. Pure composer mechanics (677w -> ~100w paragraphs, 30+ H3, a summary table, killing the meta-opener). It is also the single highest-confidence move in the whole project. +0.15 x 0.14 = +0.021.

SUM: 0.4382 + 0.029 + 0.038 + 0.030 + 0.021 = ~0.556 single compose. Best-of-3 composes adds roughly +0.008. POINT ESTIMATE ~0.564.

Against a target of ~0.556 +/- 0.019 — which is a REGRESSION ESTIMATE, not a measured number, because cellcog has no task-72 row — that is a win by less than one sigma. P(best-of-3 > 0.5578) ~ 60-65%. I land where Sol landed, and I will not dress it up any better than he did. BUT I GET THERE WITH HIS LARGEST NAMED RISK (35%, forward traversal, "an infrastructure fix, not a writing fix") STRUCTURALLY REMOVED RATHER THAN MITIGATED — AND WITH A SYSTEM THAT ALSO ANSWERS THE OTHER 99 TASKS.

THE ONE NUMBER THAT WOULD CHANGE MY ESTIMATE: the cellcog-ablation experiment (design section 6). If stripping cellcog's 2024+ citations costs it less than ~0.01, my recency thesis is worth far less than I have booked — and I want that number BEFORE we build, not after.</expected_gain>

This is precisely why the cellcog-ablation experiment must run FIRST — it is the only proposal on the table that prices a feature before we build the machine to produce it.", "REFERENCE-LIST DEPOSIT RATE IS A HARD FLOOR ON THE VOTE. Measured: only 17 of 40 unfiltered seeds (42%) carry deposited reference lists in Crossref. Adding filter=has-references:true lifts it to ~83% — but that filter SELECTS FOR PUBLISHERS WHO DEPOSIT (Elsevier, Wiley, Springer) and silently biases the derived canon toward them, away from society journals and non-Western publishers who do not. The vote therefore measures 'most-cited BY REF-DEPOSITING PUBLISHERS', not 'most-cited'. I do not know how large this bias is and I have not measured it.", "CO-CITATION IS A POPULARITY MEASURE. It reliably finds the canon; it will systematically MISS the contrarian paper, the recent null, and the heterodox account that make a review insightful rather than merely correct. Mitigations exist (empty matrix cells drive targeted search; the contradiction index hunts disagreement) but the vote's own gradient points at CONSENSUS — the direction AWAY from the heaviest-weighted dimension. This is a real tension in my design; I have fenced it, not resolved it.", "THE CONCENTRATION THRESHOLD (~0.25) IS CALIBRATED ON n=4 RUNS I DID TONIGHT (59% / 29% / 28% / 13%). It is a heuristic with a sample size of four, not a law. There is certainly a field with a genuinely diffuse citation structure where it fires a false alarm, and it will need recalibration across more domains.", "MY 2008 FIX CHANGED TWO THINGS AT ONCE. Concentration went 13% -> 28% from SELECT-gating the seeds AND rewriting the query with field vocabulary ('subprime mortgage securitization leverage'). I did not ablate them separately. I attribute most of the gain to SELECT, but I have NOT proven that — and the query-vocabulary half depends on an LLM guessing a field's terms correctly, which is a weaker link than I would like.", "THE VOTE OPENS A FABRICATION CHANNEL THAT DID NOT EXIST BEFORE. A derived anchor is a DOI and a vote count — a paper we have never read. If vote rank alone licenses prose ('the seminal study in this field shows...'), we assert a finding we never saw, via a clever mechanism, which makes it worse and not better. Gated by 'vote rank licenses attention, never assertion' — but this is a NEW risk that I introduced, and it must be tested adversarially, not assumed closed.", "COVERAGE-MATRIX SATURATION MAY BE UNREACHABLE AND WE WILL NOT KNOW UNTIL WE RUN IT. Sol wants >=80% of 80 cells with >=2 numbered studies each — that is 128+ well-grounded studies. If the literature simply does not contain two numbered journal studies of AI's wage effects in agriculture, that cell can only ever be a DECLARED_GAP: honest, and worth something, but not the coverage the arithmetic booked.", "CROSS-DIMENSION BLEED MAY DOMINATE EVERY LEVER HERE, IN EITHER DIRECTION. The judge scores all ~25 criteria in ONE comparative call and writes the comparative analysis BEFORE the numbers. If all four dimensions land within 0.01 of each other and all are ~0.01-0.02 short, no retrieval fix reaches it (Sol's X3, and he is right to name it) — it would mean sentence-level prose quality is the binding constraint and the entire retrieval investment is necessary but not sufficient.", "EVEN IF EVERYTHING WORKS, THE MARGIN IS UNDER ONE SIGMA. Point estimate ~0.564 against a target of ~0.556 +/- 0.019 that IS NOT A MEASURED NUMBER — cellcog has no task-72 row; it is a regression estimate whose residual SD (0.0094) is exactly our smallest resolvable effect. We may beat the estimate and lose to the truth. Nothing in my design fixes that, and no plan submitted tonight can."]</honest_risks>

input.thesis:
The canon is not searched for and not hand-named — it is the FIXED POINT OF CO-CITATION FROM THE FRONTIER. Keyword search fails on canonical papers because their titles use their era's vocabulary (Autor-Levy-Murnane 2003 contains neither "AI" nor "labor market"), but it succeeds on RECENT papers precisely because their titles use today's vocabulary — so we date-filter-search the frontier, harvest the frontier's reference lists, and let them VOTE; the winners of that vote ARE the canon, derived at runtime, for any field. This inverts the one broken graph edge (forward traversal, dead on this box) into the one that works (backward references, 200 OK), retiring Sol's 35% risk and Fable's 100%-COCI dependency in a single move — and every remaining constant (scope, matrix, outline, stopping rule) is generated from the prompt by the judge's own criteria generator.</thesis>
<design">STRAY</design>
<design>ARCHITECTURE: DERIVE SCOPE -> SEARCH FRONTIER (date-filtered) -> SELECT (LLM, vs the actual question) -> VOTE (co-citation) -> EXPAND (backward refs) -> SELECT -> WEIGHT -> DEDUP -> CONSOLIDATE -> STOP (matrix saturation). The stage that is NEW is VOTE. The stage that is in the wrong PLACE in both plans is SELECT.

== 1. THE INVERSION ==
/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_build.py:8-14 states a true observation, draws a false conclusion, and hardcodes its way around it: "WHY NOT KEYWORD SEARCH: tried and rejected... Crossref sorted by relevance returns papers with the keywords IN THE TITLE, which are the LOW-QUALITY ones... The canonical papers do not have 'artificial intelligence labor market employment' verbatim in their titles. WHAT ACTUALLY WORKS: ... We already hold those anchors (Frey & Osborne, Autor, Acemoglu & Restrepo...)."

Every observation is TRUE. The conclusion is BACKWARDS. Keyword search is bad at finding the CANON and good at finding the FRONTIER, for one and the same reason: TITLE VOCABULARY TRACKS PUBLICATION DATE. The docstring found the frontier and threw it away because it was hunting the canon. Sol's STEP 1 ("15 canonical anchors, hand-named, not searched") and Fable's "1-2 hand-verified anchors PER MATRIX CELL" are the same surrender — the overfit the operator caught in FIX 4.

THE FRONTIER IS THE BALLOT BOX. Recent papers cite the canon. So: (1) keyword-search WITH A DATE FILTER; (2) pull each hit's reference list from Crossref (200 OK); (3) count DOIs; (4) the top of that distribution IS the canon.

I RAN THIS TONIGHT. Query "artificial intelligence labor market employment wages displacement", filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01, rows=40. 17 seeds had deposited reference lists; 296 distinct cited DOIs. The vote, verbatim:
  x5  10.1016/j.techfore.2016.08.019  Frey & Osborne, TFSC 2017
  x5  10.1162/003355303322552801      Autor, Levy & Murnane, QJE 2003  <-- the paper "keyword search PROVABLY cannot find"
  x4  10.1002/smj.3286                Felten, Raj & Seamans, SMJ
  x3  10.1257/aer.103.5.1553          Autor & Dorn, AER 2013
  x3  10.1257/jep.33.2.3              Acemoglu & Restrepo, JEP 2019
  x3  10.1257/pandp.20181019          Brynjolfsson, Mitchell & Rock  <-- bodhi's source [1]
  x3  10.1086/705716                  Acemoglu & Restrepo, JPE 2020
  x3  10.1086/718327                  Acemoglu & Restrepo            <-- bodhi's source [3]
  x2  10.1126/science.adj0998         Eloundou et al., Science 2024
  x2  10.1257/aer.20160696            Acemoglu & Restrepo, AER
  x2  10.1257/jep.29.3.3              Autor, JEP 2015
  x1  10.1257/jel.40.1.7              Acemoglu, JEL
ELEVEN OF SOL'S FIFTEEN HAND-NAMED ANCHORS, DERIVED IN ~40 SECONDS FROM NOTHING BUT THE TASK PROMPT — including ALM 2003, the paper SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:98-105 and OPERATOR FIX 4 both cite as proof keyword search cannot find the canon. It cannot. Co-citation from the frontier can. It also rediscovered both sources behind bodhi's winning sentence. The anchor list is now DERIVED.

== 2. RECENCY COMES FROM SEARCH, NOT TRAVERSAL (measured) ==
Operator FIX 1, re-verified: crossref /works?query.bibliographic=generative+AI+labor+market+employment&filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01 -> HTTP 200 in 1.56s, total-results 278,427. Our corpus has ZERO works after 2023. cellcog mentions 2023/2024/2025 164 times; bodhi 11; cellcog is #1. The largest measured deficit in the brief is closed by a query string — not infrastructure, not a proxy, a query string.

BOTH PLANS GOT THE DIRECTION WRONG, NOT THE EFFORT. They needed 2003-anchors -> 2024-papers, i.e. the CANON->RECENT edge, i.e. forward traversal, i.e. the dead one. Channels measured by me tonight:
- Crossref /works/{doi} reference[] (BACKWARD): 200 OK. ALM 2003 -> 80/80 refs returned. is-referenced-by-count 4743.
- Crossref date-filtered search: 200 OK, 278k results, 1.5s.
- OpenAlex single-entity /works/doi:{doi}: 200 OK.
- OpenAlex list/filter (?filter=cites:): 429 Rate limit exceeded.
- Semantic Scholar graph API: 404.
- OpenCitations COCI (FORWARD): 200 — BUT ONLY AFTER A 301. Fable's URL is STALE: opencitations.net/index/coci/api/v1 redirects to api.opencitations.net/index/v1. Follow it: 1,334 citing DOIs for JEP 2019, 819 of them 2024+. Body also arrives truncated on a plain read (Fable's IncompleteRead); needs a streaming retry.

We do not need the dead edge. We start at the frontier and walk BACKWARD — the direction that returns 200. SOL'S GATE 0 (blocking preflight: "20 consecutive HTTP 200 forward-citation lookups, or forward expansion is declared dead and the kill rule arms") IS DELETED AS A BLOCKING GATE. COCI forward survives, DEMOTED to a FILL tool aimed at specific empty matrix cells, used AFTER we know which cells are empty. Sol priced forward-traversal failure at 35% and "a bodhi-class outcome". That risk is not mitigated. It is STRUCTURALLY GONE.

== 3. SELECT IS THE BROKEN STAGE — I proved it by breaking my own pipeline ==
CLINICAL — "CAR-T therapy versus bispecific antibodies relapsed refractory multiple myeloma", from 2024-01-01. Same code, zero changes:
  x10 10.1038/s41591-023-02528-9     Elranatamab (MagnetisMM-3), Nature Medicine
  x9  10.1056/nejmoa2203478          Teclistamab (MajesTEC-1), NEJM
  x9  10.1056/nejmoa2204591          Talquetamab GPRC5D (MonumenTAL-1), NEJM
  x8  10.1056/nejmoa2024850          Ide-cel (KarMMa), NEJM
  x7  10.1016/s0140-6736(21)00933-8  Cilta-cel (CARTITUDE-1), Lancet
  x5  10.1038/s41591-023-02491-5     Mechanisms of antigen escape from BCMA/GPRC5D
  x5  10.1126/scitranslmed.aau7746   GPRC5D is a target for immunotherapy of myeloma
Exactly the pivotal trials plus the mechanism papers. A myeloma specialist would name that list. Nothing in the code knows what myeloma is.

HISTORICAL — "causes of the 2008 global financial crisis", from 2015-01-01. IT FAILED:
  x2  10.1093/ww/9780199540884.013.257752  Llewellyn of Steep, Baron  <-- A WHO'S WHO ENTRY
  x2  10.5040/9781474230636.0017           Geoffrey Milne 1898-1942   <-- AN OBITUARY
  x2  10.4337/9781800370890.00012          Professor Tim Congdon CBE
  x2  10.3167/ip.2004.200112               The Collapse of Parmalat
Garbage. And the failure HAS A NUMBER: top anchor = 2 votes from 15 ref-bearing seeds = 13% concentration, vs 59% (myeloma) and 29% (AI/labour).

The bug was in MY OWN PROBE, and it was exactly operator FIX 2: I HARVESTED REFERENCE LISTS FROM UNSELECTED SEEDS. Books, encyclopedias and obituaries voted. I moved SELECT before the vote — a structural gate on the seeds (real ISSN-bearing journal container, deposited reference list) plus a query carrying the field's vocabulary — and re-ran with nothing else changed. Concentration 13% -> 28%:
  x14 10.1162/qjec.2009.124.4.1449  Mian & Sufi, QJE 2009 — Consequences of Mortgage Credit Expansion
  x11 10.1162/qjec.2010.125.1.307   Keys, Mukherjee, Seru & Vig, QJE 2010 — Did Securitization Lead to Lax Screening?
  x8  10.1093/rfs/hhp033            Demyanyk & Van Hemert, RFS — Understanding the Subprime Mortgage Crisis
  x7  10.1016/j.jfineco.2010.04.003 Piskorski, Seru & Vig, JFE
  x6  10.1257/jep.23.1.27           Mayer, Pence & Sherlund, JEP — The Rise in Mortgage Defaults
The canon of the 2008 literature, derived. SELECT-BEFORE-EXPAND IS NOT HYGIENE; IT IS THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN MIAN-SUFI AND AN OBITUARY. Sol runs SELECT after expansion; Fable runs it as stage (e) of eight, after the graph is built. Both let the junk vote.

== 4. THE SELF-DIAGNOSTIC — what makes this a system, not a demo ==
concentration = (votes for top anchor) / (selected, ref-bearing seeds). A RUNTIME signal, computable with NO ground truth, that tells the system whether its own retrieval worked. Measured tonight: 59% (myeloma), 29% (AI/labour), 28% (2008 selected), 13% (2008 unselected = the failure). Coherent seeds -> a few works are co-cited by a large fraction of the frontier. Junk seeds -> votes scatter, max collapses to 1-2.

ESCALATION LADDER instead of a silent bad corpus:
- concentration >= ~0.25 -> the field has a canon and we found it. EXPAND.
- concentration < ~0.25 -> DO NOT PROCEED. Reformulate: widen the date window (the 2008 literature peaks 2010-2015, not 2024+ — the window is derived from the question's temporal locus, not a constant); regenerate queries from the FIELD's vocabulary rather than the USER's phrasing; drop ISSN-less seeds; re-vote.
- still low after N reformulations -> the field genuinely has no citation canon (true for some policy/industry questions). The system SAYS SO in the Scope section and falls back to breadth-first date-filtered search with the LLM SELECT gate carrying the whole load. An honest degraded mode, declared in prose.
Neither plan has a retrieval stage that can tell you it failed. Both would have shipped the obituary and blamed the composer.

== 5. THE FULL CHAIN, WITH FILE:LINE ==
STAGE 0 — DERIVE SCOPE. New: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/scope.py. Input: the task prompt and NOTHING ELSE. Fields, all derived by one LLM call: topic; recency_window (task 72 -> 2024-01-01; "2008 crisis" -> ~2010-01-01, the literature's locus, not today) as a HARD ADMISSION GATE per operator FIX 3, not Sol's "recency-bonus for >=2023" (a weight cannot keep 2019 papers out of a corpus that needs 2025 ones); venue_class (task 72's prompt literally says "Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles" -> peer_reviewed_journal_only — READ FROM THE PROMPT, not typed by an engineer); language; query_seeds (8-15 strings spanning the field's vocabulary).

WHY venue_class MUST BE DERIVED — I MEASURED THE TRAP. Top 100 Crossref results for our own query under filter=type:journal-article:
  16  SSRN Electronic Journal  <-- A PREPRINT SERVER PASSING A JOURNAL-ARTICLE FILTER
   3  Selected Issues Papers
   2  IMF Working Papers
   2  IZA World of Labor
SSRN ELECTRONIC JOURNAL CARRIES ISSN 1556-5068. So Fable's "ISSN allowlist" does not save you, and a hardcoded blocklist of {SSRN, NBER} is just more overfit. The general test is structural (volume + issue + pages + non-repository DOI prefix + real container ISSN) COMBINED with the derived venue_class. And the point that settles the argument: THE SAME RULE THAT MUST EXCLUDE NBER FOR TASK 72 MUST INCLUDE IT FOR "WHAT CAUSED THE 2008 FINANCIAL CRISIS", where NBER and Fed research are PRIMARY sources and excluding them is a scholarly error. A hardcoded venue rule is wrong on one of those two questions no matter how you set it. Only derivation is right on both.

STAGE 0b — DERIVE THE COVERAGE MATRIX. New: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/matrix.py. The operator's keystone (FIX 4), mechanism verified at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py: four generators (generate_eval_criteria_prompt_comp / _insight / _instruction / _readability) plus generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt. EVERY ONE IS .format(task_prompt=...) — handed the task text and NOTHING ELSE. Each returns JSON {criterion, explanation, weight} summing to 1.0. We run the grader's own generator on the same prompt and get the same ~25 criteria the judge grades us against; matrix axes are read from THOSE criteria, not off task 72's rubric by hand as Sol does.
BONUS NEITHER DESIGNER NOTICED: generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt PROVES THE DIMENSION WEIGHTS ARE ALSO GENERATED PER-TASK. INSIGHT .32 / COMPREH .29 / INSTR .25 / READ .14 is not a property of RACE — it is task 72's generated weight vector. The file's own example_2 yields COMPREHENSIVENESS 0.40 / INSIGHT 0.15 for a data-overview task. A SYSTEM THAT HARDCODES "INSIGHT IS HEAVIEST, SPEND THERE" IS OVERFIT TO TASK 72 IN THE ONE PLACE NOBODY WAS LOOKING. Generate the weights at runtime; let them say where to spend.
Cell state: EMPTY | THIN (1 card) | FILLED (>=2 independent works, each with a number and a stated design) | DECLARED_GAP (corpus-scoped).

STAGE 1 — SEARCH THE FRONTIER. New: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/frontier.py. Crossref query.bibliographic x Scope.query_seeds x filter=type:journal-article,has-references:true,from-pub-date:{recency_window}. Adding has-references:true is MEASURED to lift the ref-bearing rate from 17/40 (42%) to ~50/60 (83%) — it triples the ballot box for free. Polite pool with mailto.

STAGE 2 — SELECT. Rewire, don't rewrite: /home/polaris/wt/compose/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/topic_relevance_gate.py. It EXISTS and is already the right shape: _build_batch_prompt(research_question, ...) at :261 batches candidates to an LLM judge against the actual question. ITS OWN DOCSTRING, LINE 8, ALREADY SAYS WHAT THE OPERATOR HAD TO REDISCOVER: "tier system rates CREDIBILITY, not RELEVANCE, so a credible-but-irrelevant [source passes]". The codebase already knows. It sits behind topic_gate_enabled() (:57) and is NOT ON THE CRITICAL PATH — the identical failure pattern as validate(). Wire it on, hand it Scope, and it becomes the ONLY admission authority. Prestige becomes a WEIGHT, never a FILTER. This is what keeps ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist and the 1974 reading-automaticity paper out.

STAGE 3 — VOTE. New: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/canon_vote.py. Fetch reference[] for every SELECTED seed; count DOIs; emit ranked anchors + concentration + the escalation ladder. These are the anchors. Nobody typed them.

STAGE 4 — EXPAND. Backward (primary): Crossref reference[] from the derived anchors, depth 2 — reuses journal_corpus_build.py's working machinery; only its anchor SOURCE changes, from a hand list to the vote. Forward (fill only): COCI at api.opencitations.net/index/v1/citations/{doi} (follow the 301; streaming retry), fired ONLY at anchors whose matrix cells are still EMPTY — 819 of JEP-2019's 1,334 citers are 2024+, a precision instrument for a named hole, not a foundation. DELETE scripts/deep_fetch.py:78-88 (S2 fallbacks: 404 today; they silently return nothing while inflating the perceived fetch budget). DELETE all OpenAlex list/filter calls (429). KEEP OpenAlex single-entity (200) purely for abstract recovery where Crossref has none.

STAGE 5 — SELECT AGAIN. Same gate, same Scope. ONE admission authority for the whole system.

STAGE 6 — WEIGHT (ranks WITHIN scope; never admits). co_citation_votes x topic_relevance_score x venue_tier x recency x groundability. The vote is a BETTER canonicity signal than raw is-referenced-by-count because it is RELEVANCE-CONDITIONED: it counts citations FROM THE ON-TOPIC FRONTIER, not from all of science. ResNet has ~200k citations and ZERO votes here — exactly why it is in our corpus today and would not be under this design.

STAGE 7 — DEDUP / CONSOLIDATE. DOI-canonical + title-similarity clustering merging WP/journal version pairs into ONE work carrying the journal identity and the WP text (Sol's working-paper text lane, kept). Fix the lying full-text detector in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/journal_corpus_fetch.py (operator FIX 7.3: it accepted a 548-word Oxford landing page reading "CONTACT Name Email... We welcome feedback" as FULLTEXT for Frey & Osborne): require >=1500 words AND title-in-body AND no landing-page chrome.

STAGE 8 — STOP. When the matrix stops filling, not when a counter hits 100. Halt when (a) every cell is FILLED or DECLARED_GAP, (b) two consecutive expansion rounds add <3 newly-filled cells, (c) concentration >= threshold. Empty cells at halt are not failures — they are the FUTURE-RESEARCH AGENDA, measured rather than asserted. Sol had this idea; it now runs on a matrix nobody typed.

== 6. THE CHEAPEST VISIBILITY-TO-VALUE EXPERIMENT (operator FIX 6) — RUN BEFORE BUILDING ANY OF THE ABOVE ==
Fable named the load-bearing unknown: we have proven the judge can SEE our scholarship; we have not proven it PAYS. Both plans answer it by building everything and composing once. That is a three-week question. WE CAN ANSWER IT IN ONE AFTERNOON FOR ~20 JUDGE CALLS AND ZERO BUILD, BECAUSE WE ALREADY HOLD THE #1 ARTIFACT. Take cellcog's task-72 report (/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/cellcog-max.jsonl, id=72) and ablate a document that already scores at the top:
  A: cellcog verbatim (control)
  B: every 2024+ citation swapped for a pre-2023 one -> PRICES RECENCY (the 164-vs-11 gap; my entire angle)
  C: the 13 epistemic tags stripped -> PRICES THE TAG TAXONOMY
  D: the 8 adjudication syntheses flattened into plain reporting -> PRICES ADJUDICATION (the 0.32-weight bet)
Score each k=5 (SD 0.0074; paired resolution +0.0094). This measures THE JUDGE'S PRICE FOR EACH FEATURE on a document whose score is already anchored, BEFORE we spend an hour building the machine that produces them. If the judge does not pay for recency, my retrieval angle is worth far less than I have booked, and I want to know on day one, not day twenty. Nobody proposed pricing a feature on someone else's artifact. It is nearly free.

== 7. THE THREE CODE BUGS — re-verified, all real ==
1. validate() IS IMPORTED AND NEVER CALLED. grep -n "validate(" /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py -> line 49 "from synthesis_contract import Premise, Synthesis, validate, OPERATIONS" and ZERO call sites in the file. The only thing between LLM prose and the page is _clean(), a regex strip, which the composer then PRINTS as "sentences DROPPED by the contract". The safety contract is theatre. Commits 225b323 ("mechanism never operated") and b3acb72 ("the judge fell off the critical path") are this same bug, twice. Fix: structured sentence ledger; every sentence through validate(); a CI test asserting a poisoned card yields zero poisoned sentences on the page.
2. cellcog_composer.py:167 — verified verbatim: 'mechanisms': f.get('mechanisms') or [], sits three lines below the span gate at :158-161 that DOES check claim and span against source text. An LLM-invented mechanism string becomes a global citation license (two-hop laundering). Every mechanism carries its own verbatim span or it dies.
3. The full-text detector (journal_corpus_fetch.py) — a lying measurement, fixed at Stage 7.

== 8. WHAT RETRIEVAL OWES THE COMPOSER ==
Retrieval does not score. It removes the ceiling. It hands the composer four things it cannot otherwise have: 2024+ JOURNAL EVIDENCE (we have zero; cellcog leans on it 164 times); a CONTRADICTION INDEX (Fable's idea — but a contradiction index over 70 mostly-off-topic works is noise, while one over a matrix-saturated on-topic corpus is the raw material for cellcog's highest-value section); MEASURED GAPS (the empty cells); and an AUDITABLE PROVENANCE STORY that makes the Scope-and-Methods section true rather than asserted.</design>
<why_general>I did not argue this. I RAN it — same code, same constants, three domains, tonight.

DERIVED AT RUNTIME (nothing below is typed by an engineer):
- topic <- the prompt (LLM scope extraction)
- recency window <- the prompt (LLM: where is this literature's temporal locus?)
- venue class <- the prompt (LLM reads the instruction)
- language <- the prompt
- query seeds <- the prompt (LLM generates field vocabulary)
- COVERAGE MATRIX AXES <- the prompt, via RACE's own criteria_prompt_en.py, .format(task_prompt=...)
- DIMENSION WEIGHTS <- the prompt, via RACE's own generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt
- ANCHOR PAPERS <- the corpus, via co-citation vote from the date-filtered frontier
- outline <- the matrix
- stopping condition <- the matrix (cells filled; marginal yield < 3)
- retrieval trust <- the vote (co-citation concentration)

HARDCODED — THE COMPLETE LIST: rows=40..60 per query; depth=2; concentration_threshold ~= 0.25; >=2 works to FILL a cell; >=1500 words = FULLTEXT; two consecutive rounds <3 new cells -> stop. SIX NUMBERS. NOT ONE MENTIONS AI, LABOUR, MYELOMA, OR 2008. They are tuning parameters of a graph search, not facts about a field. Compare Sol and Fable: 15 named papers, a 10-section outline, an 8x10 matrix, a topic regex, a list of 10 industries — all facts about ONE question.

WALKTHROUGH 1 — "CAR-T vs bispecific antibodies in relapsed myeloma" (CLINICAL)
SCOPE (derived): topic = comparative efficacy/safety of BCMA/GPRC5D-directed CAR-T vs T-cell-engaging bispecifics in RRMM. recency = 2024-01-01 (fast-moving therapeutic area). venue_class = THE PROMPT DOES NOT SAY "JOURNAL ARTICLES ONLY", so it derives WIDER: peer-reviewed journals PLUS pivotal trial reports PLUS regulatory labels — because in oncology those are primary evidence. THIS IS THE OPPOSITE OF TASK 72'S DERIVED VENUE CLASS, PRODUCED BY THE SAME CODE.
MATRIX (from RACE's generator, given only this prompt): criteria along the lines of depth of efficacy comparison; safety/toxicity (CRS, ICANS, infection); patient-population coverage (prior lines, high-risk cytogenetics, extramedullary disease); sequencing and resistance; access and manufacturing constraints. Rows and columns fall out of THAT. Nobody at POLARIS types "CRS" or "ICANS".
FRONTIER -> VOTE (measured tonight): MagnetisMM-3 (x10), MajesTEC-1 (x9), MonumenTAL-1 (x9), KarMMa (x8), CARTITUDE-1 (x7), the GPRC5D-target paper, the antigen-escape mechanism paper. Concentration 59% — the highest of the three domains, because oncology has an unusually tight canon of pivotal trials. The system proceeds with high confidence, correctly.
EXPAND: backward from CARTITUDE-1/KarMMa reaches the phase-1 dose-escalation studies and the BCMA-biology papers; COCI forward from KarMMa fills the "sequencing after CAR-T failure" cell with 2025 work; SELECT kills the vision-transformer and radiomics papers any date-filtered oncology query drags in.
CONTRADICTION INDEX: CAR-T's deeper, more durable responses vs bispecifics' off-the-shelf availability and lower early mortality — cards that genuinely diverge, with dimension diffs on population (prior lines) and horizon (durability). That is the adjudication, and it is the SAME MACHINERY that resolves China-firm-level vs Finnish-population-level in the labour question.

WALKTHROUGH 2 — "What caused the 2008 financial crisis" (HISTORICAL)
SCOPE (derived): recency = ~2010-01-01, NOT 2024. THIS IS THE CASE THAT KILLS A HARDCODED DATE FLOOR: the 2008 literature peaked 2009-2015, and a 2024+ filter returns retrospectives and pedagogy, not primary research. The window is a derived property of the question's temporal locus.
venue_class (derived): journals PLUS NBER/Fed/IMF working papers, which are PRIMARY sources here. A HARDCODED "NO WORKING PAPERS" RULE — WHICH SOL PROPOSES, AND WHICH TASK 72'S PROMPT GENUINELY REQUIRES — WOULD BE A SCHOLARLY ERROR ON THIS QUESTION. One rule cannot be right for both. Only derivation is.
FRONTIER -> VOTE: I ran this twice, and THE FIRST RUN FAILED — concentration 13%, top anchors a Who's Who entry and an obituary for Geoffrey Milne (1898-1942). THIS IS THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT RESULT IN MY REPORT, because it shows the system has a failure mode AND CAN NAME IT AT RUNTIME. With SELECT applied to the seeds before the vote, concentration rose to 28% and the canon appeared: Mian & Sufi QJE 2009 (x14), Keys-Mukherjee-Seru-Vig QJE 2010 (x11), Demyanyk & Van Hemert RFS (x8), Piskorski-Seru-Vig JFE (x7), Mayer-Pence-Sherlund JEP (x6). That is the literature.
HONEST CAVEAT I WILL NOT HIDE: my selected 2008 run skewed toward the securitization/mortgage sub-literature, because my query said "subprime mortgage securitization leverage". That is the query driving the vote — correct behaviour, but incomplete. In the real system the query seeds come from THE MATRIX (which for this question generates cells for global imbalances, monetary policy, ratings agencies, shadow banking, regulatory capture), so multiple queries produce multiple sub-canons and THE MATRIX — NOT MY HAND — enforces balance across the competing causal accounts. That is exactly the mechanism Sol built for industries; I am generalising it to whatever axes the judge's own generator names.

THE PROOF: the three questions share ZERO domain constants. AI/labour derived Autor-Levy-Murnane. Myeloma derived MajesTEC-1. 2008 derived Mian-Sufi. Same code, same six numbers; the only input that changed was the prompt. Sol's and Fable's plans would each need a new hand-built anchor list, a new hand-read matrix and a new topic regex per question — three separate engineering projects. That is the difference between a research system and an expensive way to answer one question.</why_general>
<faithfulness_position>THE LINE: FABRICATION = putting words in a source's mouth. SCHOLARSHIP = our own reasoning, marked as such. The moat is NOT "every sentence must be span-grounded" — it is "no sentence may put words in a source's mouth." I adopt the operator's formulation exactly and Fable's three refinements without amendment, because Fable's decode MEASURED that 71% of the #1 system's sentences carry no citation and that it stays honest by LABELLING, not by refusing to think.

WHAT WE KEEP (this is what protects a research product people TRUST):
1. No number, entity, study, or attribution that is not in an admitted extractive span. Non-negotiable. A 0.60 obtained by fabricating is a 0.00.
2. THE MECHANISM POOL IS THE UNION OF THE SENTENCE'S OWN CITED CARDS — never the corpus. Fable proved a corpus-wide pool degenerates the substring test at synthesis_contract.py:197-205 into "does this sentence contain a word the field uses", and FLIPS OUR OWN ATTACK #1 FROM REJECT TO PASS. ATTACK #1 stays REJECTED as a CI canary on every commit. bodhi never needed the widening: its bridge sentence already cites the mechanism's paper.
3. HEDGE + TAG ALONE IS COSTUME, NEVER A LICENSE. An analytical synthesis must be a CONDITIONAL over >=2 ADMITTED premises carrying premise_ids, so the no-new-number and no-new-entity gates STILL RUN underneath it.
4. GAP CLAIMS ARE CORPUS-SCOPED. cellcog writes "No peer-reviewed study has yet measured X" — a false universal it cannot know. We write "Within the 137 journal articles retrieved for this review, none measures X." True by construction; identical judge-visible payoff.
5. Every mechanism carries its OWN verbatim span (kills cellcog_composer.py:167).
6. validate() ON THE CRITICAL PATH, with a poisoned-card CI test. This bug has shipped twice. Never again.

WHAT WE DROP (costs score, protects nothing):
1. The entailment_judge.py:588 NEUTRAL clause ("delete any sentence introducing a fact, entity, mechanism or specificity NOT present in the SPAN"). It deletes every cross-source inference BY CONSTRUCTION, on the heaviest-weighted dimension, and it is why 98% of our sentences are bare facts. Replaced by the typed contract — NOT by nothing.
2. The single-span union entailment gate. It kills bodhi's winning sentence, whose two components are each fully grounded in the two papers it cites.
3. The blanket working-paper reject as a CORPUS INPUT rule. Keep the ban on CITING non-journals when the prompt demands journals (task 72 does, w=0.15); drop it for TEXT SUBSTRATE. Unpaywall cannot reach the econ canon (ALM 2003 is_oa=false; AER 2018's OA URL is a 403 Cloudflare wall), but NBER/IZA carry the same paper. Cite the journal version; span-check against the WP text; NEVER quote a number from a WP PDF (version drift is real). And note: for the 2008 question the DERIVED venue class admits working papers as citable — and that is correct.
4. The hard-coded abstract at cellcog_composer.py:400-414, which asserts "draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" regardless of what the corpus actually contains. THAT IS A FABRICATED COMPLIANCE CLAIM AIMED SQUARELY AT THE INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING GRADER — fabrication with a score motive, and the worst single thing in our codebase. The abstract is derived from admitted cards; the Scope section may state only what a corpus audit proves at compose time.

THE NEW RULE MY OWN DESIGN FORCES (nobody has this, and I introduced the risk):
The co-citation vote opens a fabrication channel that did not previously exist: IT CAN PROMOTE A PAPER WE HAVE NEVER READ. A derived anchor is a DOI plus a vote count — that is ALL it is. If "Autor, Levy and Murnane (2003), in the QJE, showed that..." reaches prose on the strength of vote rank alone, we have asserted a finding we never saw. That is fabrication arrived at through a clever mechanism, which makes it worse, not better.
RULE: VOTE RANK LICENSES ATTENTION, NEVER ASSERTION. An anchor must pass fetch -> select -> card-extraction with a verbatim span before one word about it reaches the page. Vote rank is a retrieval priority. It is never evidence.
COROLLARY — CANONICITY CLAIMS ARE CORPUS-SCOPED TOO. We may not write "the seminal study in this field." We write "the most frequently cited work among the 214 reference lists surveyed for this review" — measured, true by construction, and something no other system on the board can say.

WHERE RETRIEVAL PROVENANCE BECOMES SCORE: the RACE cleaner deletes markers and bibliographies; leaderboard_gpt55.csv shows citation_accuracy and effective_citations as "-" for all ten systems. THE JUDGE CANNOT SEE OUR PROVENANCE — EXCEPT IN PROSE. cellcog's section 1.2 "Scope, Methods, and Source Selection" narrates its selection criteria and pre-empts the working-paper objection in prose; its instruction-following is 0.5530 against our 0.4409. A DERIVED scope makes that section true and specific: we can state the derived recency window, the query set, the vote, the concentration, the matrix saturation and the declared gaps. CORPUS-SCOPED HONESTY AND INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING SCORE ARE THE SAME SENTENCE. That is the one place where doing the scholarship properly and scoring well are not in tension — and it is the whole reason the faithfulness line is worth defending rather than merely tolerating.</faithfulness_position>
<builds_on>TAKEN FROM SOL:
- THE COVERAGE MATRIX AS THE STOPPING CONDITION, not a paper count. Correct and load-bearing. I changed only where the axes come from (generated, not hand-read).
- THE THREE HONEST EVIDENCE TIERS (QUOTABLE / ABSTRACT-CITABLE / NAMED) with all three counts published. His insight that ABSTRACT_ONLY is a first-class tier — because the judge reads PROSE, never a PDF, and the unit prose needs is "author + journal + year + a number + a design", which economics abstracts carry — is genuinely good and I keep it whole. It is also what makes my date-filtered frontier immediately usable: Crossref abstracts are free and they scale.
- THE WORKING-PAPER TEXT LANE (cite the journal version, span-check against the NBER/IZA PDF, never quote a number from it). This is how the econ canon becomes quotable at all.
- THE THREE CODE BUGS. All three independently verified by me. All three fixed.
- THE REFUSAL TO LOWER THE BAR. Gate at ~0.567, not bodhi's 0.5441.
- PER-TURN DISCIPLINE: score per-dimension, spend the next turn entirely on the largest deficit, never stack levers (commit fb228dd is the evidence).

TAKEN FROM FABLE:
- THE cellcog DECODE. It is the most valuable artifact in this project, and I used it to OVERRULE SOL TWICE. cellcog's real citation form is "Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal*, find that..." (73 instances; 160 narrative year-parens), NOT Sol's contorted "Writing in the QJE in 2003, Autor, Levy and Murnane show...", which nobody on the board uses. And cellcog's spine is THREE TECHNOLOGY ERAS, not 4IR.
- THE FOUR SENTENCE CLASSES (REPORT / COMPARE / SCOPE-DIFFERENCE / ANALYTICAL SYNTHESIS with premise_ids). The correct shape of the synthesis lane.
- MECHANISM POOL = THE SENTENCE'S OWN CITED CARDS, with ATTACK #1 as a CI canary. Fable is right; Sol's corpus-wide pool would have flipped our own fabrication test to PASS.
- CORPUS-SCOPED GAP CLAIMS. True by construction, same payoff.
- EXTRACTIVE CARDS ONLY — no LLM-written field may serve as the ground-truth span. The LLM may SELECT offsets, never COMPOSE spans.
- THE CONTRADICTION INDEX. The adjudication move is the #1 system's highest-value section, and this is the only proposed machinery that can reproduce it from verified cards.
- NAMING THE LOAD-BEARING UNKNOWN (visibility vs value). I built the cheap experiment her honesty implies.

REJECTED FROM SOL:
- THE 15 HAND-NAMED ANCHORS (STEP 1). Replaced by the co-citation vote, which derived 11 of his 15 in 40 seconds — including the one he correctly said keyword search cannot find. He was right about the problem and wrong that it forced hand-naming.
- GATE 0: FORWARD-CITATION PREFLIGHT AS A BLOCKING GATE, with its 35% "sinks the plan" risk. Deleted. We never need the forward edge to reach recent work — WE START THERE. This is the largest change I make to his plan, and it removes his own #1 named failure mode.
- "RECENCY-BONUS FOR >=2023" AS A WEIGHT. A weight cannot keep 2019 papers out of a corpus that needs 2025 ones. Recency is a derived HARD ADMISSION GATE (operator FIX 3).
- THE 4IR SPINE — his single biggest composer bet (11.5% of score). Fable's decode REFUTES it from the artifact: cellcog mentions the prior industrial revolutions in ONE sentence, explicitly demotes the frame ("this review treats 4IR as a useful periodization but declines to adopt it uncritically"), and cites peer-reviewed CRITIQUES of it. THE SYSTEM THAT DOES BUILD THE FULL FOUR-REVOLUTION COMPARISON TABLE IS WHALECLOUD — AT 0.5396. Sol's spine copies the 0.54 pack, not #1. Take Fable's critique-and-subordinate instead.
- FORMAT-D ATTRIBUTION. Measured: contorted, and cellcog never does it.
- THE 8x10 MATRIX READ OFF TASK 72'S RUBRIC BY HAND, and the hand-listed 10 industries. Generated.

REJECTED FROM FABLE:
- ">=35% OF GROUNDABLE CARDS DATED 2024+ IS 100% DEPENDENT ON THE COCI FORWARD CHANNEL." False, and it was the scariest sentence in her plan. It is dependent on a Crossref date filter that returns 278,427 journal articles in 1.5 seconds. She bet the entire recency pillar on the one channel that needs a 301-follow and a streaming retry, when a plain search does it.
- "1-2 HAND-VERIFIED CANONICAL ANCHORS PER MATRIX CELL, found by Crossref relevance + human check." This is Sol's overfit with a human in the loop — MORE hand-work, not less, and it does not scale to a second question, let alone 100.
- THE SSRN/NBER CONTAINER-TITLE QUARANTINE AS A NAMED BLOCKLIST. Right instinct, wrong mechanism. Measured: SSRN Electronic Journal carries ISSN 1556-5068 and passes filter=type:journal-article (16 of the top 100 hits), so an ISSN allowlist does not catch it — and a two-name blocklist is more hardcoding that would wrongly exclude NBER from the 2008-crisis question, where it is a primary source.
- SELECT AS STAGE (e) OF EIGHT, AFTER THE GRAPH IS BUILT. I broke my own pipeline in exactly this way and got an obituary as a top anchor. SELECT runs BEFORE expansion, or the junk votes.

WHAT NEITHER OF THEM FOUND:
1. THE FRONTIER-TO-CANON INVERSION. Both stared at "keyword search cannot find the canon" and concluded "so we must name it by hand." The frontier was sitting in the search results they threw away.
2. THE CO-CITATION CONCENTRATION SELF-DIAGNOSTIC. Neither plan's retrieval stage can tell you it failed.
3. THE DIMENSION WEIGHTS ARE GENERATED PER-TASK TOO (generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt). "INSIGHT is heaviest at 0.32" is a fact about task 72, not about RACE. Both plans treat it as a constant — overfit in the one place nobody was looking.
4. THE CHEAP VISIBILITY-TO-VALUE EXPERIMENT — ablate cellcog's OWN artifact, score k=5. ~20 judge calls, zero build.
5. "VOTE RANK LICENSES ATTENTION, NEVER ASSERTION" — the fabrication channel my own mechanism opens, gated before it exists.</builds_on>
